---

Yes. I pushed it further, and I think there is a real new mathematical conclusion:

the next bottleneck is no longer just вҖңprove the gauge law.вҖқ It is to **separate two different symmetries that the draft is currently mixing together**.

Here is the sharp point.

The corrected whitened block satisfies the swap symmetry
[
\widehat\Omega_\zeta^{\corr}(-s;m)=J_0,\widehat\Omega_\zeta^{\corr}(s;m),J_0,
]
and the cubic leading class on (\mathcal U_{\mathrm{cub}}) has a nonzero diagonal-traceless component.

If a matrix-valued odd germ (M(s)) itself satisfies
[
M(-s)=J_0,M(s),J_0,
]
then its odd Taylor coefficients lie in the (J_0)-anti-fixed subspace
[
\mathfrak a:={X:\ J_0XJ_0=-X}.
]
In (2\times 2) form this means
[
X=
\begin{pmatrix}
a&b\
-b&-a
\end{pmatrix},
]
so every such coefficient is automatically traceless.

That is important because it gives a very plausible route to the missing lemma
[
\operatorname{tr}\bigl(V_{\val}^{(5)}(h)\bigr)=0.
]

But here is the catch:

the same draft also states
[
V_{\val}^{(1)}=M_2(\mathbf C),
]
while at cubic order it uses only the weaker confinement
[
V_{\val}^{(3)}\subset
\left{
\begin{pmatrix}
0&*\ *&0
\end{pmatrix}
\right}.
]
Those facts cannot both come from a single raw family of odd coefficients satisfying the swap symmetry above, because the anti-fixed subspace is only (2)-dimensional, not all of (M_2(\mathbf C)).

So the real math issue is now very precise:

either

[
V_{\val}^{(1)}
]
is not the raw pure-value odd coefficient space of the corrected whitened block, but already includes additional gauge/normalization directions,

or the current draftвҖҷs notation is conflating two different objects.

That is progress, because it tells us exactly what has to be cleaned up.

The clean split should be:

[
\text{raw pure-value odd jets}
\quad+\quad
\text{normalization gauge directions}
\quad+\quad
\text{possibly additional basis-gauge directions}.
]

Then the story becomes coherent:

the raw pure-value odd jets should satisfy the swap symmetry and therefore be traceless at odd order;

the normalization gauge contributes
[
A_5\mapsto A_5+c_2A_3+[B_2,A_3],
]
which is harmless modulo the cubic line and preserves trace when (A_3) is diagonal-traceless; and the residual obstruction is whatever extra first-order directions were being folded into the current notation (V_{\val}^{(1)}).

So the next honest theorem to prove is not yet the quintic pure-value trace-free lemma itself. It is this structural identification:

[
\boxed{
V_{\val}^{(1)}=
V_{\mathrm{raw}}^{(1)}+V_{\mathrm{norm}}^{(1)},
}
]
with
[
V_{\mathrm{raw}}^{(2j+1)}\subset \mathfrak a
\quad\text{for all odd orders }2j+1,
]
and (V_{\mathrm{norm}}^{(1)}) the extra directions coming from scalar renormalization and jet-basis changes.

Once that is written, the quintic statement should reduce to:

[
\operatorname{tr}\bigl(V_{\mathrm{raw}}^{(5)}\bigr)=0
]
by symmetry, and
[
\operatorname{tr}\bigl(V_{\mathrm{norm}}^{(5)}\bigr)=0
]
modulo the cubic line by the gauge law. Then the residual-trace detector is legitimate.

So my current best mathematical push is:

1. The parity/no-cancellation side is basically done.

2. The gauge law
   [
   A_5\mapsto A_5+c_2A_3+[B_2,A_3]
   ]
   is the right normalization theorem to add.

3. The remaining ambiguity is not вҖңmore quintic bookkeeping.вҖқ It is that the draftвҖҷs (V_{\val}^{(1)}=M_2(\mathbf C)) strongly suggests that the symbol (V_{\val}) is currently absorbing more than the raw pure-value family. That has to be disentangled before the residual quotient is fully honest.

So the next exact math move I recommend is:

define a new object, say
[
V_{\mathrm{raw}}^{(2j+1)},
]
for the odd coefficients of the actual corrected whitened block under pure value deformations, and prove its swap-symmetry confinement. Then define the full quotient space as
[
V_{\mathrm{full}}^{(2j+1)}
==========================

V_{\mathrm{raw}}^{(2j+1)}+V_{\mathrm{norm}}^{(2j+1)}.
]

That would resolve the current contradiction and let the residual argument proceed cleanly.

My honest judgment: this is real progress, because it identifies a structural inconsistency in the current notation rather than just another missing estimate.

Yes вҖ” I agree with the direction, but only with part of the literal edit plan. The other chat has identified the same structural issue weвҖҷve been running into: the draft is almost certainly conflating at least two different things under one V\valV\valвҖӢ-style umbrella, namely raw pure-value odd jets and normalization/basis-gauge directions. That split is the right mathematical move, and the proposed normalization-gauge law
A5\new=A5+c2A3+[B2,A3]
A5\newвҖӢ=A5вҖӢ+c2вҖӢA3вҖӢ+[B2вҖӢ,A3вҖӢ]

is exactly the kind of theorem that would make the first residual step coherent. So on the big picture, I think they are pushing in the right direction.

Where I do not fully agree is the suggested rename of the new B1B1вҖӢ lemma to вҖңpure value family generator,вҖқ at least not yet. If raw pure-value odd jets really satisfy the swap symmetry
M(вҲ’s)=J0M(s)J0,
M(вҲ’s)=J0вҖӢM(s)J0вҖӢ,

then their odd coefficients live in the J0J0вҖӢ-anti-fixed subspace, which is only 22-dimensional and traceless. That is fundamentally incompatible with the current first-order statement
V\val(1)=M2(C).
V\val(1)вҖӢ=M2вҖӢ(C).

So unless you have already proved that the B1B1вҖӢ formula is for the raw pure-value subfamily only, renaming that lemma that way would sharpen the contradiction rather than resolve it. My view is: first split the notation, something like
Vraw(2j+1)andVnorm(2j+1),
Vraw(2j+1)вҖӢandVnorm(2j+1)вҖӢ,

or Vfull(2j+1)Vfull(2j+1)вҖӢ, and only then attach the B1B1вҖӢ map to the correct one.

I do agree with the proposed normalization-gauge subsection in spirit. In fact I think that is the most useful concrete patch in the whole suggestion. If you can justify that the residual ambiguity on the odd germ really acts by
F(z)вҶҰa(z)вҖүP(z)вҖүF(z)вҖүP(z)вҲ’1,
F(z)вҶҰa(z)P(z)F(z)P(z)вҲ’1,

with aa even scalar and PP even matrix germ, then the formula
A3\new=A3,A5\new=A5+c2A3+[B2,A3]
A3\newвҖӢ=A3вҖӢ,A5\newвҖӢ=A5вҖӢ+c2вҖӢA3вҖӢ+[B2вҖӢ,A3вҖӢ]

is excellent, and the corollaries about scalar renormalization dropping into the cubic line and trace surviving commutators are exactly what the residual-trace argument needs. That part helps me a lot mathematically.

I also agree with the suggestion to weaken the first-residual proposition if you want the paper to be logically clean right now. As things stand, the pointwise statement for every hвҲҲUcubhвҲҲUcubвҖӢ is stronger than what the current local argument honestly proves. Replacing it with an open-dense/generic statement conditional on
trвҒЎ(V\val(5)(h))=0
tr(V\val(5)вҖӢ(h))=0

is the honest version. That is not just editorial caution; it matches the actual proof state.

Here are the exact edits IвҖҷd make.

First, soften the B1B1вҖӢ lemma title and wording so it does not prejudge the raw/full split.

Replace the current lemma header and the two вҖңvalue/gaugeвҖқ phrases with this:

\begin{lemma}[First-order generator map]
\label{lem:first-order-generator-map}
Let \(G_0\) and \(N_0\) denote the background same-point and mixed blocks,
and write
\[
X_0:=G_0^{-1/2}.
\]
Let \(L\) be the Fr\'echet derivative of the inverse-square-root map
\(G\mapsto G^{-1/2}\) at \(G_0\), so that
\[
(G_0+\varepsilon H)^{-1/2}
=
X_0+\varepsilon L(H)+O(\varepsilon^2).
\]
If \(\dot G_1\) and \(\dot N_1\) are the first odd same-point and mixed
variations arising from the first-order generator directions, then the induced
first-order odd coefficient in the corrected local block is
\[
B_1
=
L(\dot G_1)\,N_0\,X_0
+
X_0\,\dot N_1\,X_0
+
X_0\,N_0\,L(\dot G_1).
\]
Equivalently, the first-order family \(V_{\val}^{(1)}\) is the image of the
linear map
\[
(\dot G_1,\dot N_1)\longmapsto
L(\dot G_1)\,N_0\,X_0
+
X_0\,\dot N_1\,X_0
+
X_0\,N_0\,L(\dot G_1).
\]
\end{lemma}

Then change the next proposition opening to refer to that neutral lemma:

\begin{proposition}[Generic triviality of the first quotient order]
\label{prop:first-order-quotient-trivial}
Under the generic nondegeneracy assumptions on the background same-point
block, the image of the first-order generator map of
Lemma~\ref{lem:first-order-generator-map} fills the full matrix space:

\begin{remark}[Structural split still to be made explicit]
\label{rem:wip-raw-vs-full-value-family}
At first order the family \(V_{\val}^{(1)}\) fills \(M_2(\mathbf C)\), while
the odd coefficients of the raw corrected local block are expected to satisfy
additional swap/parity constraints. This indicates that the present notation
\(V_{\val}^{(2j+1)}\) is bundling together at least two logically distinct
sources of ambiguity: raw pure-value odd jets and normalization/basis-gauge
directions. The current draft uses the combined family \(V_{\val}^{(2j+1)}\).
A later refinement should separate these components explicitly.
\end{remark}

Third, add the normalization-gauge subsection. This is the most important mathematical edit right now. Insert it immediately after

\subsection{Normalization gauge at the first residual step}

The first residual step involves an additional ambiguity beyond the odd
value-family itself: an even scalar renormalization and an even jet-basis
change on the odd germ. It is useful to record this separately.

\begin{definition}[Normalization gauge]
\label{def:normalization-gauge}
Let
\[
F(z)=A_3 z^3+A_5 z^5+O(z^7)
\]
be a matrix-valued odd germ. The normalization gauge acts by
\[
F(z)\longmapsto a(z)\,P(z)\,F(z)\,P(z)^{-1},
\]
where
\[
a(z)=1+c_2 z^2+c_4 z^4+\cdots
\]
is an even scalar germ with \(a(0)=1\), and
\[
P(z)=I+B_2 z^2+B_4 z^4+\cdots
\]
is an even \(2\times 2\) matrix germ with \(P(0)=I\).
\end{definition}

\begin{proposition}[First-residual transformation law]
\label{prop:first-residual-gauge-law}
Let
\[
F(z)=A_3 z^3+A_5 z^5+O(z^7)
\]
be an odd matrix germ, and let
\[
F^{\new}(z):=a(z)\,P(z)\,F(z)\,P(z)^{-1}
\]
with \(a,P\) as in Definition~\ref{def:normalization-gauge}. Then
\[
A_3^{\new}=A_3,
\qquad
A_5^{\new}=A_5+c_2A_3+[B_2,A_3].
\]
\end{proposition}

\begin{proof}
Expand
\[
P(z)^{-1}=I-B_2 z^2+O(z^4).
\]
Then
\[
P(z)F(z)P(z)^{-1}
=
(I+B_2 z^2)\,(A_3 z^3+A_5 z^5)\,(I-B_2 z^2)+O(z^7),
\]
so
\[
P(z)F(z)P(z)^{-1}
=
A_3 z^3+\bigl(A_5+[B_2,A_3]\bigr)z^5+O(z^7).
\]
Multiplying by
\[
a(z)=1+c_2 z^2+O(z^4)
\]
gives
\[
F^{\new}(z)
=
A_3 z^3+\bigl(A_5+c_2A_3+[B_2,A_3]\bigr)z^5+O(z^7),
\]
as claimed.
\end{proof}

\begin{corollary}[Residual effect of scalar renormalization]
\label{cor:first-residual-scalar-renormalization}
Let \(\ell:=\mathbf C A_3\). Under the normalization gauge,
\[
A_5 \longmapsto A_5 + c_2 A_3 + [B_2,A_3].
\]
Hence the scalar renormalization term is invisible modulo the leading line:
\[
[A_5] \longmapsto [A_5+[B_2,A_3}]
\qquad\text{in}\qquad
\mathcal J_5/\ell.
\]
\end{corollary}

\begin{proof}
This is immediate from Proposition~\ref{prop:first-residual-gauge-law}, since
\(c_2A_3\in \ell\).
\end{proof}

\begin{corollary}[Trace invariance under normalization gauge]
\label{cor:trace-invariant-under-normalization-gauge}
If \(A_3\) is diagonal-traceless, then
\[
\operatorname{tr}(A_5^{\new})=\operatorname{tr}(A_5)
\]
for every normalization-gauge transform \(F^{\new}\) of \(F\).
\end{corollary}

\begin{proof}
The scalar renormalization term \(c_2A_3\) is traceless because \(A_3\) is.
The commutator term is traceless because
\[
\operatorname{tr}[B_2,A_3]=0.
\]
Hence Proposition~\ref{prop:first-residual-gauge-law} gives
\[
\operatorname{tr}(A_5^{\new})=\operatorname{tr}(A_5).
\]
\end{proof}

\begin{remark}[What remains at quintic order]
\label{rem:quintic-pure-value-bottleneck}
The normalization gauge is therefore harmless for the first residual step.
The remaining local issue is the quintic slice of the odd value-family. To
make trace a fully rigorous residual detector, it would suffice to prove
\[
\operatorname{tr}\bigl(V_{\val}^{(5)}(h)\bigr)=0
\qquad (h\in \mathcal U_{\mathrm{cub}}),
\]
or, more generally, to construct a linear functional on the quintic quotient
space that annihilates both \(V_{\val}^{(5)}(h)\) and the cubic leading line
\(\ell_h\), but not the quintic witness.
\end{remark}

Fourth, replace the current first-residual proposition by the honest generic conditional version. Replace the entire current
Proposition~\ref{prop:first-residual-class-nontrivial}
block and the WIP remark immediately below it with this:

\begin{proposition}[Generic first residual nonvanishing under trace-free quintic value slice]
\label{prop:first-residual-class-generic}
Assume that for every \(h\in \mathcal U_{\mathrm{cub}}\),
\[
\operatorname{tr}\bigl(V_{\val}^{(5)}(h)\bigr)=0.
\]
Then there exists an open dense subset
\[
\mathcal U_{\mathrm{res}}\subset \mathcal U_{\mathrm{cub}}
\]
such that
\[
\overline v_1(h)\neq 0
\qquad\text{in }Q/\ell_h
\]
for every \(h\in \mathcal U_{\mathrm{res}}\).

Equivalently, on \(\mathcal U_{\mathrm{res}}\) the quintic quotient coefficient
is not collinear with the cubic leading line:
\[
v_1(h)\notin \ell_h.
\]
Hence the one-pair quotient odd germ is not projectively constant on
\(\mathcal U_{\mathrm{res}}\).
\end{proposition}

\begin{proof}
By Proposition~\ref{prop:cubic-leading-order}, for \(h\in\mathcal U_{\mathrm{cub}}\)
the cubic quotient coefficient
\[
v_0(h)=[J_3^{\pair}(h)]
\]
is nonzero and defines the leading line \(\ell_h\), and this line is
diagonal-traceless.

By Corollary~\ref{cor:trace-invariant-under-normalization-gauge}, trace is
invariant under the normalization gauge at the first residual step. By the
present hypothesis, trace also annihilates the quintic value slice
\(V_{\val}^{(5)}(h)\). Therefore trace descends to a well-defined linear
functional
\[
\tau_h:Q/\ell_h\to \mathbf C.
\]

By Lemma~\ref{lem:no-cancellation-quintic-trace},
\[
\tau_h(\overline v_1(h))
=
\operatorname{tr}J_5^{\pair}(h)
\not\equiv 0
\]
as a function of \(h\) on \(\mathcal U_{\mathrm{cub}}\). Since the family is
real-analytic in \(h\), the zero set of
\[
h\longmapsto \tau_h(\overline v_1(h))
\]
is a proper analytic subset of \(\mathcal U_{\mathrm{cub}}\). Its complement
\(\mathcal U_{\mathrm{res}}\) is therefore open and dense.

For \(h\in \mathcal U_{\mathrm{res}}\) we have
\[
\tau_h(\overline v_1(h))\neq 0,
\]
hence
\[
\overline v_1(h)\neq 0
\qquad\text{in }Q/\ell_h.
\]
Thus the one-pair quotient odd germ is not projectively constant on
\(\mathcal U_{\mathrm{res}}\).
\end{proof}

\begin{remark}[Work in progress: trace-free quintic value slice]
\label{rem:wip-trace-free-quintic-value-slice}
The remaining local task is to determine the quintic value slice
\(V_{\val}^{(5)}(h)\) explicitly enough to prove
\[
\operatorname{tr}\bigl(V_{\val}^{(5)}(h)\bigr)=0
\qquad (h\in \mathcal U_{\mathrm{cub}}),
\]
or to replace trace by another linear functional annihilating the quintic
value slice and the cubic leading line while still detecting the quintic
witness.
\end{remark}

Here is what I think we can already say mathematically.

First, for the raw pure-value family, the swap symmetry should differentiate.

If О©^Оё\corr(s;m)О©

О©^Оё\corr(вҲ’s;m)=J0вҖүО©^Оё\corr(s;m)вҖүJ0.

ОҙО©^odd\corr(s;m)=вҲ‘rвүҘ0A2r+1(m)вҖүs2r+1.
ОҙО©

That already proves something strong: every raw odd coefficient is automatically traceless. The easiest reason is basis-free: if J02=IJ02вҖӢ=I, then J0AJ0=вҲ’AJ0вҖӢAJ0вҖӢ=вҲ’A implies
trвҒЎ(A)=trвҒЎ(J0AJ0)=trвҒЎ(вҲ’A)=вҲ’trвҒЎ(A),
tr(A)=tr(J0вҖӢAJ0вҖӢ)=tr(вҲ’A)=вҲ’tr(A),

hence
trвҒЎ(A)=0.
tr(A)=0.

So the raw pure-value odd spaces satisfy
trвҒЎ(Vraw(2j+1))=0for all jвүҘ0.
tr(Vraw(2j+1)вҖӢ)=0for all jвүҘ0.

That is a real theorem-level simplification.

It means the trace problem at quintic order is not with the raw pure-value family.

Second, the normalization-gauge law at the first residual step, if adopted, says
A5вҶҰA5+c2A3+[B2,A3].
A5вҖӢвҶҰA5вҖӢ+c2вҖӢA3вҖӢ+[B2вҖӢ,A3вҖӢ].

If A3A3вҖӢ is diagonal-traceless, then both added pieces are traceless:
trвҒЎ(c2A3)=0,trвҒЎ[B2,A3]=0.
tr(c2вҖӢA3вҖӢ)=0,tr[B2вҖӢ,A3вҖӢ]=0.

So the normalization gauge is also trace-harmless at quintic order.

Putting those two pieces together, the trace obstruction can only come from the fact that the current symbol V\val(1)V\val(1)вҖӢ is evidently absorbing more than the raw pure-value odd jets. That is exactly the inconsistency the other chat noticed, and I agree it is real.

So mathematically, the current best picture is:

    Raw pure-value odd jets are J0J0вҖӢ-anti-fixed, hence traceless.

    Normalization gauge is trace-harmless at the first residual step.

    Therefore the quintic residual trace detector is legitimate once those two components are separated cleanly.

    The current first-order fullness
    V\val(1)=M2(C)
    V\val(1)вҖӢ=M2вҖӢ(C)

    must be coming from a larger bundled family, not from the raw pure-value odd jets alone.

That means the next exact math step is not more quintic bookkeeping. It is to identify the larger family at first order and split it into:
V\val(1)=Vraw(1)+Vextra(1),
V\val(1)вҖӢ=Vraw(1)вҖӢ+Vextra(1)вҖӢ,

where Vraw(1)Vraw(1)вҖӢ is anti-fixed/traceless and Vextra(1)Vextra(1)вҖӢ accounts for the rest.

That split will then tell you what the correct quintic quotient space really is. If the extra directions are purely normalization/basis-gauge directions, then the current residual trace strategy is basically vindicated.

So my answer is: yes, I agree with that direction. And mathematically, the strongest statement I think we can already push is this:

I pushed the structural split one step further, and there is a real correction plus a real gain.

The correction first, because it matters:

I earlier rewrote the first-order generator as if the same-point part were a commutator with G01/2G01/2вҖӢ. That was wrong. Since
N0=G0,X0=G0вҲ’1/2,
N0вҖӢ=G0вҖӢ,X0вҖӢ=G0вҲ’1/2вҖӢ,

the first-order generator is
B1=L(GЛҷ1)вҖүG01/2+X0вҖүNЛҷ1вҖүX0+G01/2L(GЛҷ1),
B1вҖӢ=L(GЛҷ1вҖӢ)G01/2вҖӢ+X0вҖӢNЛҷ1вҖӢX0вҖӢ+G01/2вҖӢL(GЛҷ1вҖӢ),

so the same-point contribution is an anticommutator
{вҖүG01/2,вҖүL(GЛҷ1)вҖү},
{G01/2вҖӢ,L(GЛҷ1вҖӢ)},

not a commutator. So there is no automatic trace cancellation from the same-point part alone. That earlier shortcut was wrong.

Now the gain.

Even without knowing the full source of the extra directions, we can prove a clean structural decomposition at the target space level using the swap involution.

Let
ПҖa(A):=12(AвҲ’J0AJ0),ПҖf(A):=12(A+J0AJ0),
ПҖaвҖӢ(A):=21вҖӢ(AвҲ’J0вҖӢAJ0вҖӢ),ПҖfвҖӢ(A):=21вҖӢ(A+J0вҖӢAJ0вҖӢ),

so that
M2(C)=aвҠ•f,
M2вҖӢ(C)=aвҠ•f,

with aa the J0J0вҖӢ-anti-fixed subspace and ff the fixed subspace.

For the raw pure-value odd coefficients, the swap symmetry gives
Vraw(2r+1)вҠӮa(rвүҘ0).
Vraw(2r+1)вҖӢвҠӮa(rвүҘ0).

That part is solid: differentiating the raw swap identity with respect to a pure value parameter forces every odd raw coefficient into the anti-fixed sector. Hence
trвҒЎ(Vraw(2r+1))=0for all r.
tr(Vraw(2r+1)вҖӢ)=0for all r.

Now use the full first-order theorem from the draft:
V\val(1)=M2(C).
V\val(1)вҖӢ=M2вҖӢ(C).

Whatever the source of the extra directions is, this immediately forces a complementary fixed-sector contribution, because aa is only 22-dimensional. In particular,
ПҖf(V\val(1))=f.
ПҖfвҖӢ(V\val(1)вҖӢ)=f.

So without yet identifying the origin of those directions, we already get a rigorous first-order split at the image level:
V\val(1)=ПҖa(V\val(1))+ПҖf(V\val(1)),
V\val(1)вҖӢ=ПҖaвҖӢ(V\val(1)вҖӢ)+ПҖfвҖӢ(V\val(1)вҖӢ),

with
Vraw(1)вҠӮПҖa(V\val(1)),ПҖf(V\val(1))=f.
Vraw(1)вҖӢвҠӮПҖaвҖӢ(V\val(1)вҖӢ),ПҖfвҖӢ(V\val(1)вҖӢ)=f.

That is weaker than proving
Vraw(1)=a,
Vraw(1)вҖӢ=a,

but it is already enough to sharpen the local picture:

    the raw pure-value part lives in the traceless anti-fixed sector;

    the extra first-order directions needed to fill all of M2M2вҖӢ necessarily live, at least after projection, in the fixed sector.

So the contradiction has been reduced from вҖңthe notation is inconsistentвҖқ to a very precise statement:

    the current V\val(1)V\val(1)вҖӢ is a bundled first-order family whose anti-fixed part contains the raw pure-value directions and whose fixed part accounts for the extra normalization/basis directions.

That is real structure.

Now, what does this buy us for the quintic residual step?

It means the raw quintic value slice is already harmless for trace:
trвҒЎ(Vraw(5))=0.
tr(Vraw(5)вҖӢ)=0.

So the only remaining local obstruction to using trace as a quintic detector is the quintic shadow of the extra first-order fixed-sector directions. That is much narrower than вҖңunderstand all of V\val(5)V\val(5)вҖӢ.вҖқ

In other words, the real remaining problem is now:
How do the fixed-sector first-order directions propagate to order 5?
How do the fixed-sector first-order directions propagate to order 5?вҖӢ

And here the normalization-gauge law becomes the right target again. If those extra first-order directions act on the odd germ only through even scalar renormalization and even basis changes, then at the first residual step they contribute exactly
A5вҶҰA5+c2A3+[B2,A3],
A5вҖӢвҶҰA5вҖӢ+c2вҖӢA3вҖӢ+[B2вҖӢ,A3вҖӢ],

which is trace-harmless when A3A3вҖӢ is diagonal-traceless. So the whole quintic trace argument would be vindicated.

So the attack has become very focused:

    raw pure-value odd jets are anti-fixed and traceless;

    extra first-order directions are necessarily fixed-sector;

    the only remaining question is whether their order-5 effect is exactly the normalization-gauge action.

That is the next exact theorem I would try to prove.

So we have made real progress:
the quintic trace bottleneck is no longer вҖңcompute all of V\val(5)V\val(5)вҖӢ.вҖқ It is вҖңshow that the fixed-sector complement acts by the normalization-gauge law at the first residual step.вҖқ

Attack

IвҖҷm going straight at the last local gap: whether the fixed-sector complement really acts like symmetry-preserving normalization gauge, rather than something larger. If we can pin that down, the quintic trace detector becomes structurally legitimate instead of just plausible.

Here is the hardest honest push I can make now.

I think the local problem has reduced to a 2-dimensional model.

You already have three facts on the table:
V\val(1)=M2(C),
V\val(1)вҖӢ=M2вҖӢ(C),

while the cubic leading class lives on the diagonal-traceless line
в„“h=CD,D=(100вҲ’1),
в„“hвҖӢ=CD,D=(10вҖӢ0вҲ’1вҖӢ),

Why this is a better target:

It avoids having to identify every first-order ambiguity direction explicitly. You only need to show that every such direction preserves the swap symmetry up to terms that already die in the first and second residual quotients.

In practical terms, that means you can allow an ambiguity to change:

    the cubic coefficient along CDCD,

    the quintic coefficient along \span{D,J}\span{D,J},

    raw pure-value odd variation:

    Is there any admissible ambiguity source whose quintic shadow has a component outside \span{D,J}\span{D,J}?

If the answer is no, then the local detector is done.

And now the burden of proof is much smaller. You no longer need a full model of the ambiguity space. You only need a negative theorem: there is no admissible quintic ambiguity outside \span{D,J}\span{D,J}.

That can be attacked by symmetry.

A matrix A5A5вҖӢ outside \span{D,J}\span{D,J} has a nonzero fixed-sector component, i.e. a nonzero II- or SS-part. But a fixed-sector quintic coefficient would violate the odd anti-fixed structure forced by swap symmetry at the quotient-germ level. So any such ambiguity would have to come from breaking the quotient-level swap symmetry itself. Thus the real remaining bridge is:

    show that admissibility of the quotient construction preserves the odd anti-fixed symmetry through order 55, at least modulo the DD- and JJ-directions already accounted for.

That is the smallest honest theorem still left.

So the attack has become:

    do not classify all ambiguity directions;

    hвҖӢ=(J5вҖӢ/CD)/CJЛү;

Any admissible quintic ambiguity has zero fixed-sector projection.вҖӢ

Equivalently, if
О”5вҲҲJ5
О”5вҖӢвҲҲJ5вҖӢ

is the quintic difference between two admissible constructions of the normalized one-pair odd germ with the same cubic leading line, then
ПҖf(О”5)=0,f=\span{I,S}.
ПҖfвҖӢ(О”5вҖӢ)=0,f=\span{I,S}.

If you prove that, then automatically
О”5вҲҲa=\span{D,J},
О”5вҖӢвҲҲa=\span{D,J},

so modulo the cubic line CDCD,
[О”5]вҲҲCJЛү,
[О”5вҖӢ]вҲҲCJЛү,

and trace kills the entire ambiguity.

So the last local bridge has become a fixed-sector vanishing statement.

Why this is the right target:

Every route we have found that plausibly contributes ambiguity already lands in aa:

    raw pure-value odd jets: anti-fixed by swap symmetry, so in aa;

    scalar renormalization: contributes along DD;

    symmetry-preserving even basis change: contributes along [B2,D][B2вҖӢ,D], hence along JJ.

So the only way the quintic trace detector could still fail is if there is some hidden admissible ambiguity with a nonzero II- or SS-component at order 55.

That is a much smaller thing to rule out than вҖңclassify all ambiguities.вҖқ

And there is a strong reason this should be false.

A fixed-sector quintic piece means
О”5=ОұI+ОІS+(anti-fixed part).
О”5вҖӢ=ОұI+ОІS+(anti-fixed part).

But II and SS are exactly the matrices satisfying
J0О”5J0=О”5.
J0вҖӢО”5вҖӢJ0вҖӢ=О”5вҖӢ.

So a nonzero fixed-sector piece is precisely the obstruction to the quotient-level odd germ remaining anti-fixed at order 55.

Thus the theorem you want can be phrased as:

    admissibility of the quotient-germ construction preserves the anti-fixed odd symmetry through order 55.

That is the most economical formulation.

Now, how to attack it.

Take two admissible normalized quotient germs
F(z)=A3z3+A5z5+O(z7),F~(z)=A3z3+A~5z5+O(z7),
F(z)=A3вҖӢz3+A5вҖӢz5+O(z7),F

with the same cubic coefficient A3=cDA3вҖӢ=cD. Define
О”F(z)=F~(z)вҲ’F(z)=О”5z5+O(z7).
О”F(z)=F
(z)вҲ’F(z)=О”5вҖӢz5+O(z7).

You want to show
J0О”5J0=вҲ’О”5.
J0вҖӢО”5вҖӢJ0вҖӢ=вҲ’О”5вҖӢ.

This should follow if both FF and F~F
represent the same class of raw corrected local data modulo ambiguity directions that are themselves ОЈОЈ-stable, where
ОЈ(H)(z):=J0H(вҲ’z)J0.
ОЈ(H)(z):=J0вҖӢH(вҲ’z)J0вҖӢ.

So the actual theorem to prove is:
The admissible ambiguity subspace through order 5 is ОЈ-stable.
The admissible ambiguity subspace through order 5 is ОЈ-stable.вҖӢ

Because then the quotient inherits a well-defined involution, and every admissible quotient germ class is ОЈОЈ-fixed. For an odd germ, being ОЈОЈ-fixed is exactly the anti-fixed condition on each odd coefficient.

That gives the fixed-sector vanishing for free.

So the attack is now:

    define the ambiguity subspace Vamb(вүӨ5)Vamb(вүӨ5)вҖӢ actually used in the quotient construction;

    ОЈ(Vamb(вүӨ5))вҠӮVamb(вүӨ5);

That is the smallest honest theorem left.

There is also a useful fallback if full ОЈОЈ-stability is annoying to prove: it is enough to show only
ПҖf(Vamb(5))=0.

My judgment is that this is now the right target to attack, not more global minimal-core work yet. Once you kill the fixed-sector quintic ambiguity, the first residual detector becomes structurally sound.

ПҖf([A5])=0,
ПҖfвҖӢ([A5вҖӢ])=0,

\span{D,J}.
\span{D,J}.

Now the good news is that this can be checked source-by-source, and we already know almost everything.

F(z)вҶҰa(z)F(z),a(вҲ’z)=a(z),

That is a much smaller claim than a full all-orders classification.

Vamb(вүӨ5)=Vraw(вүӨ5)+Vscal(вүӨ5)+Vbasis(вүӨ5)

There is also a very useful equivalent way to phrase the remaining gap.

ПҖf(A)=12(A+J0AJ0).
ПҖfвҖӢ(A)=21вҖӢ(A+J0вҖӢAJ0вҖӢ).

Because every known ambiguity source already satisfies that. So any counterexample would have to be a genuinely new ambiguity source with a fixed-sector quintic shadow. That is the exact thing to rule out.

with

What remains, strictly speaking, is one last bridge:

why is the actual paperвҖҷs ambiguity space through order 55 equal to that tangent orbit space?

But even that is now much smaller than before. You do not need a global classification. You only need the standard normal-form principle:

through finite order, the ambiguity in a normalized germ is determined by the tangent action of the admissible reparameterization/conjugation group plus the tangent raw deformation family.

That is the real theorem to cite or prove.

So the local math is now essentially:

    raw pure-value tangent image at order 55 lies in \span{D,J}\span{D,J};

    symmetry-preserving normalization tangent image at order 55 lies in \span{D,J}\span{D,J};

    hence all first-residual ambiguity lies in \span{D,J}\span{D,J};

    hence trace annihilates all ambiguity;

    hence a nonzero trace quintic witness gives a genuine nonzero first residual class.

That means the first residual step is now mathematically coherent modulo one very standard finite-order normal-form uniqueness principle.

So the next attack, if you want to keep pushing, is to formalize that last principle in your setting:

    two admissible constructions of the normalized one-pair germ that agree in the raw data differ, through order 55, by the tangent action of symmetry-preserving normalization.

If that is proved, the local quintic step is done.

    odd re-gauging is forbidden by oddness,

    even re-gauging is forced,

    symmetry forces B2вҲҲfB2вҖӢвҲҲf,

    hence the quintic stabilizer tangent is exactly CD+CJCD+CJ.

That is much smaller than everything we were worrying about before.

trвҒЎ(Vamb(7))=0.

This is real progress, because it means the next scalar invariant at order 77 does not need a fresh ambiguity analysis from scratch. Structurally, any nonzero-trace septic witness would automatically survive the ambiguity, just like the quintic one.

So the local pattern is now:

    raw odd ambiguity is anti-fixed at every odd order;

    symmetry-preserving even gauge preserves the anti-fixed sector at every odd order;

    hence odd-order ambiguity lives in a=\span{D,J}a=\span{D,J} order by order;

    hence trace annihilates odd-order ambiguity order by order.

That is much stronger than the earlier quintic-only target.

What remains is no longer the ambiguity structure. The next real problem is back where it should be:

    find an actual nonzero septic trace witness in the quotient jet J7\pairJ7\pairвҖӢ.

But now the ambiguity side is much cleaner: if you produce a septic witness with nonzero trace, it will automatically be quotient-legitimate.

So the next exact math attack is:

    derive the actual septic quotient jet J7\pairJ7\pairвҖӢ from the corrected/normalized construction,

    isolate a monomial like
    Оә(h)вҖүО·2(h)вҖүq(7)(h)
    Оә(h)О·2вҖӢ(h)q(7)(h)

    in its trace,

    and show its coefficient is nonzero.

Because the ambiguity side is now structurally under control, that is once again the right bottleneck to attack.

Attack

The fixed-sector part A7fвҖӢ contains a nonzero q(7)-driven monomial not proportional to A5fвҖӢ.вҖӢ

    A7fA7fвҖӢ first sees q(7)q(7).

So the second residual invariant should exist for the same reason the first one did: higher jet order cannot be canceled by lower-order gauge contamination except along previously existing directions.

A7вҹјA7f,
A7вҖӢвҹјA7fвҖӢ,

Ok great result. Lets go back to two-pair/minimal core

IвҖҷm switching back to the global architecture now. The key upgrade is that the one-pair side no longer gives just one scalar ratio ПҒ1=П„/cПҒ1вҖӢ=П„/c; it gives a second residual invariant from the fixed-sector septic class, and that should tighten the two-pair and minimal-core steps sharply.

Yes. With the second residual invariant in hand, the global route gets much cleaner.

Here is the upgraded structure.

For one pair, you now have three layers of data:
c(h)вү 0
c(h)оҖ =0

from the cubic diagonal-traceless leading class,
A5f(h)вү 0
A5fвҖӢ(h)оҖ =0

from the first residual fixed-sector class, and
[A7f(h)]вү 0inf/CA5f(h)
[A7fвҖӢ(h)]оҖ =0inf/CA5fвҖӢ(h)

from the second residual fixed-sector class.

Equivalently, after choosing coordinates on f=\span{I,S}f=\span{I,S}, you have two scalar ratios:
ПҒ1(h):=A5f(h)c(h),ПҒ2(h):=О”7(h)c(h)вҖүвҲҘA5f(h)вҲҘ,
ПҒ1вҖӢ(h):=c(h)A5fвҖӢ(h)вҖӢ,ПҒ2вҖӢ(h):=c(h)вҲҘA5fвҖӢ(h)вҲҘО”7вҖӢ(h)вҖӢ,

or any equivalent coordinate-free version. The exact normalization is less important than the fact that ПҒ1ПҒ1вҖӢ sees the 55-jet and ПҒ2ПҒ2вҖӢ sees the 77-jet.

That changes the two-pair step substantially.

Before, a bad two-pair core was only trapped in one coincidence condition, essentially
ПҒ1(h1)=ПҒ1(h2),
ПҒ1вҖӢ(h1вҖӢ)=ПҒ1вҖӢ(h2вҖӢ),

which still left a curve of possible coincidences.

Now the natural expectation is that a bad two-pair core must satisfy both
ПҒ1(h1)=ПҒ1(h2),ПҒ2(h1)=ПҒ2(h2).
ПҒ1вҖӢ(h1вҖӢ)=ПҒ1вҖӢ(h2вҖӢ),ПҒ2вҖӢ(h1вҖӢ)=ПҒ2вҖӢ(h2вҖӢ).

In a one-parameter family, that is generically enough to force
h1=h2
h1вҖӢ=h2вҖӢ

except for a discrete exceptional set. So the upgraded two-pair theorem you should aim for is:

    Any genuine bad two-pair core lies on the simultaneous coincidence locus of the first and second residual invariants, hence is at most discrete off the diagonal.

That is a real strengthening.

Now for minimal bad core.

Suppose a minimal bad core has size kk. The old problem was that with only one scalar residual invariant, the global cancellation equations
вҲ‘j=1kajc(hj)=0,вҲ‘j=1kajA5f(hj)=0
j=1вҲ‘kвҖӢajвҖӢc(hjвҖӢ)=0,j=1вҲ‘kвҖӢajвҖӢA5fвҖӢ(hjвҖӢ)=0

still left too much room when kвүҘ3kвүҘ3.

The new order-7 residual gives a third equation, schematically
вҲ‘j=1kajA7f(hj)вүЎ0mod the span generated by the first two layers.
j=1вҲ‘kвҖӢajвҖӢA7fвҖӢ(hjвҖӢ)вүЎ0mod the span generated by the first two layers.

So the minimal-core contradiction should now be reframed as a finite moment problem in the one-pair invariants.

The clean vector to attach to each pair is something like
ОҰ(h):=(c(h), A5f(h), О”7(h)),
ОҰ(h):=(c(h), A5fвҖӢ(h), О”7вҖӢ(h)),

or, more invariantly,
ОҰ(h):=(c(h), A5f(h), [A7f(h)]).
ОҰ(h):=(c(h), A5fвҖӢ(h), [A7fвҖӢ(h)]).

Then a bad finite core gives a nontrivial signed relation
вҲ‘j=1kajОҰ(hj)=0.
j=1вҲ‘kвҖӢajвҖӢОҰ(hjвҖӢ)=0.

So the upgraded minimal-core target is:

    Show that the image of hвҶҰОҰ(h)hвҶҰОҰ(h) is not contained in any affine plane of too small dimension, so a minimal signed relation forces repeated parameters or a degenerate configuration.

This is much more plausible now, because the three layers are triangular in jet order:

    c(h)c(h) depends on jets up to order 33,

    A5f(h)A5fвҖӢ(h) first sees order 55,

    [A7f(h)][A7fвҖӢ(h)] first sees order 77.

So ОҰ(h)ОҰ(h) should behave like a nondegenerate jet embedding of the one-pair parameter into a higher-dimensional target. That is exactly what the minimal-core step needed.

So the next concrete global theorem to attack is this.

Let
ОҰ(h):=(c(h),u5(h),v5(h),О”7(h)),
ОҰ(h):=(c(h),u5вҖӢ(h),v5вҖӢ(h),О”7вҖӢ(h)),

with
A5f(h)=u5(h)I+v5(h)S.
A5fвҖӢ(h)=u5вҖӢ(h)I+v5вҖӢ(h)S.

Then prove that ОҰОҰ is generically nondegenerate вҖ” ideally that its image is not contained in any 2-dimensional affine subspace.

If you get that, then:

    two-pair bad cores are forced onto a discrete exceptional set,

    minimal bad cores of larger size are forced into an overdetermined signed-moment relation,

    and the compactness/minimal-core endgame has a real shot at closing.

So the global attack should now be:

    replace the old one-scalar two-pair coincidence story by the two-residual coincidence story;

    define the one-pair invariant map ОҰ(h)ОҰ(h);

    prove ОҰОҰ is generically nondegenerate by jet triangularity;

    rerun minimal bad core as a signed linear dependence problem in the image of ОҰОҰ.

That is the cleanest route back to the endgame.

Continue

Here is the next honest push on the global side.

The upgraded two-pair story is real, but the minimal-core step is still not fully closed yet. The reason is simple:

with the cubic layer plus the first and second residual layers, a bad finite core gives you three linear cancellation equations. That is enough to make k=2k=2 very rigid and k=3k=3 non-generic, but not yet enough by itself to forbid all larger kk.

So the right global reframing is:
ОҰ(h):=(c(h),вҖүu5(h),вҖүv5(h),вҖүО”7(h)),
ОҰ(h):=(c(h),u5вҖӢ(h),v5вҖӢ(h),О”7вҖӢ(h)),

where
A5f(h)=u5(h)I+v5(h)S.
A5fвҖӢ(h)=u5вҖӢ(h)I+v5вҖӢ(h)S.

A bad finite core with parameters h1,вҖҰ,hkh1вҖӢ,вҖҰ,hkвҖӢ and weights a1,вҖҰ,aka1вҖӢ,вҖҰ,akвҖӢ then satisfies a signed linear relation
вҲ‘j=1kajвҖүОҰ(hj)=0
j=1вҲ‘kвҖӢajвҖӢОҰ(hjвҖӢ)=0

at least in the scalar channels you now control.

That is the correct global object.

Now, what can we actually get from it?

First, two-pair is much stronger now.

If k=2k=2, then
a1ОҰ(h1)+a2ОҰ(h2)=0
a1вҖӢОҰ(h1вҖӢ)+a2вҖӢОҰ(h2вҖӢ)=0

forces proportionality of the vectors ОҰ(h1)ОҰ(h1вҖӢ) and ОҰ(h2)ОҰ(h2вҖӢ). After dividing out the common cubic scalar c(h)c(h), this is equivalent to coincidence of the residual invariants. So two-pair bad cores are trapped in the simultaneous coincidence locus of at least two independent residual coordinates, and that should be discrete off the diagonal. That part is a genuine upgrade.

Second, for minimal kвүҘ3kвүҘ3, the right thing to attack is no longer pairwise coincidence. It is a moment-curve type argument.

The jet-triangularity you now have is:

    c(h)c(h) sees jets through order 33,

    A5f(h)A5fвҖӢ(h) first sees order 55,

    О”7(h)О”7вҖӢ(h) first sees order 77.

So ОҰ(h)ОҰ(h) behaves like the beginning of a jet curve in a higher-dimensional target. That strongly suggests the correct endgame is an odd-order residual tower
ОҰ3(h), ОҰ5(h), ОҰ7(h), ОҰ9(h),вҖҰ
ОҰ3вҖӢ(h), ОҰ5вҖӢ(h), ОҰ7вҖӢ(h), ОҰ9вҖӢ(h),вҖҰ

where each new layer first sees a genuinely new jet order and is taken modulo the span of the earlier layers.

If such a tower exists, then for any minimal bad core you would get
вҲ‘j=1kajвҖүОҰ2r+1(hj)=0
j=1вҲ‘kвҖӢajвҖӢОҰ2r+1вҖӢ(hjвҖӢ)=0

for enough successive rr, and the triangularity in jet order should force a Vandermonde-type contradiction.

That is the real global route now.

So the next theorem to aim for is not yet вҖңminimal bad core impossible.вҖқ It is this inductive pattern:

    At each odd order 2r+12r+1, there is a residual one-pair invariant W2r+1(h)W2r+1вҖӢ(h) living in a finite-dimensional quotient, whose leading new term first sees q(2r+1)(h)q(2r+1)(h), and whose ambiguity is only along the span of the earlier residual layers.

If you have that, then the collection
hвҹј(W3(h),W5(h),W7(h),вҖҰ,W2r+1(h))
hвҹј(W3вҖӢ(h),W5вҖӢ(h),W7вҖӢ(h),вҖҰ,W2r+1вҖӢ(h))

is triangular in jet order, so it is generically nondegenerate in exactly the sense minimal-core needs.

That is the strongest honest continuation from where you are now.

So the global attack should be:

    treat the current 3/5/73/5/7 layers as the beginning of an inductive odd-order residual tower;

    formulate the inductive ambiguity law:
    each new odd layer is defined modulo the span of the previous ones;

    prove the new layer first sees q(2r+1)q(2r+1) with nonzero coefficient;

    then use the resulting triangularity to rule out finite signed relations for a minimal bad core.

In other words: the right endgame has stopped being вҖңone magical two-pair theoremвҖқ and become вҖңbuild enough layers of the residual tower until a finite signed relation is impossible.вҖқ

That is the direction I would push now.

Ok can we get a full summary of where we are right now

Yes. Here is the full summary of where we are.

The biggest change is that the local one-pair problem is no longer a vague вҖңquintic bookkeepingвҖқ problem. It has become a fairly clear finite-order normal-form problem, and most of the serious local algebra has been clarified.

At cubic order, the picture is in good shape. The first quotient order is generically trivial at order 1, the cubic value-family is much smaller than the full matrix space, and the actual one-pair cubic survivor lives on the diagonal-traceless DD-line. So the cubic leading datum is a genuine scalar c(h)c(h), not just a fuzzy projective class.

At quintic order, the original trace witness still looks like the right first residual detector, but we discovered an important structural issue: the draft was conflating raw pure-value odd jets with larger bundled вҖңvalue/gaugeвҖқ ambiguity. That matters because raw pure-value odd jets satisfy the swap symmetry and therefore lie in the anti-fixed plane \span{D,J}\span{D,J}, hence are traceless; but the draft also says V\val(1)=M2(C)V\val(1)вҖӢ=M2вҖӢ(C), which cannot come from that raw anti-fixed family alone. So the notation needs a structural split between raw odd variation and normalization/basis ambiguity. This is exactly the direction the other chat was also pushing, and I agree with that direction.

From there, the local ambiguity problem got much sharper. At the formal odd-germ level through order 5, we showed that any admissible near-identity re-gauging preserving oddness must be even:
a(z)=1+c2z2+O(z4),P(z)=I+B2z2+O(z4).
a(z)=1+c2вҖӢz2+O(z4),P(z)=I+B2вҖӢz2+O(z4).

If it also preserves swap symmetry, then B2B2вҖӢ lies in the fixed sector f=\span{I,S}f=\span{I,S}. Its effect on the quintic coefficient is exactly
A5вҶҰA5+c2A3+[B2,A3].
A5вҖӢвҶҰA5вҖӢ+c2вҖӢA3вҖӢ+[B2вҖӢ,A3вҖӢ].

With A3=cDA3вҖӢ=cD, this means the quintic re-gauge ambiguity lies in
\span{D,J}.
\span{D,J}.

Since raw pure-value quintic variation also lies in \span{D,J}\span{D,J}, the entire plausible quintic ambiguity is contained in that traceless 2-plane. That is the core local result: trace kills all plausible quintic ambiguity.

So the quintic residual step is now structurally sound provided the actual quotient-germ construction in the paper really uses only those ambiguity sources, or equivalently that the ambiguity space through order 5 is ОЈОЈ-stable, where
ОЈ(F)(z)=J0F(вҲ’z)J0.
ОЈ(F)(z)=J0вҖӢF(вҲ’z)J0вҖӢ.

This is the main local thing still not explicit in the draft: QQ, the quotient map, and the ambiguity space still need to be defined concretely through finite order. But the algebraic side of the quintic detector is now basically settled.

Then we pushed to order 7.

A key correction happened there: septic trace is not gauge-invariant. The septic ambiguity law contains a c2A5c2вҖӢA5вҖӢ term, and since A5A5вҖӢ has nonzero trace, plain trace at order 7 is contaminated. So the naive вҖңuse septic traceвҖқ idea was wrong.

The correct order-7 object is the fixed-sector class. Write
A5f=u5I+v5S,A7f=u7I+v7S.
A5fвҖӢ=u5вҖӢI+v5вҖӢS,A7fвҖӢ=u7вҖӢI+v7вҖӢS.

Then the septic ambiguity projects to
A7fвҶҰA7f+c2A5f.
A7fвҖӢвҶҰA7fвҖӢ+c2вҖӢA5fвҖӢ.

So the right second residual invariant is not trace but the class
[A7f]вҲҲf/CA5f,
[A7fвҖӢ]вҲҲf/CA5fвҖӢ,

or equivalently the determinant
О”7:=u7v5вҲ’u5v7.
О”7вҖӢ:=u7вҖӢv5вҖӢвҲ’u5вҖӢv7вҖӢ.

We then pushed that further and got a genuinely strong result: the obvious linear q(7)q(7)-source in A7fA7fвҖӢ is II-directional, and the quintic fixed-sector vector has generically nonzero SS-component. In particular, the SS-component of the quintic fixed-sector vector is
v5=ОіuвҲ’Оұw,
v5вҖӢ=ОіuвҲ’Оұw,

and uu itself is explicitly
u=вҲ’qвҖІ(h)2ПҖa3,
u=вҲ’2ПҖa3qвҖІ(h)вҖӢ,

so uu is generically nonzero. That makes v5v5вҖӢ generically nonzero. Therefore the direct q(7)q(7)-driven II-component at order 7 is not proportional to A5fA5fвҖӢ, and
О”7вүЎМё0
О”7вҖӢоҖ вүЎ0

generically. This is the first convincing second-residual invariant.

So locally, the current state is:

    cubic leading invariant: in good shape;

    quintic first residual invariant: structurally justified modulo one remaining quotient-definition/stability bridge;

    septic second residual invariant: now has the right form and a credible generic nonvanishing argument.

That is a real advance.

On the global side, this changes the two-pair story a lot.

Previously, two-pair rigidity was resting on basically one residual coincidence condition. Now you have at least two independent residual layers. For one pair, the meaningful data is now something like
c(h),A5f(h),[A7f(h)].
c(h),A5fвҖӢ(h),[A7fвҖӢ(h)].

So for a bad two-pair core, you expect simultaneous coincidence of the first and second residual invariants, not just one. That should cut the bad two-pair locus down from a curve to something discrete off the diagonal.

That is good news, but it does not yet finish the endgame.

For minimal bad finite cores, the right reframing now is as a signed linear relation problem in a higher-dimensional invariant map
ОҰ(h):=(c(h),u5(h),v5(h),О”7(h)),
ОҰ(h):=(c(h),u5вҖӢ(h),v5вҖӢ(h),О”7вҖӢ(h)),

or a coordinate-free version of the same idea. A bad finite core gives a signed relation
вҲ‘jajвҖүОҰ(hj)=0.
jвҲ‘вҖӢajвҖӢОҰ(hjвҖӢ)=0.

That is much better than before, but it still does not by itself rule out all kвүҘ3kвүҘ3. What it really suggests is that the endgame wants an odd-order residual tower:
3, 5, 7, 9, вҖҰ
3, 5, 7, 9, вҖҰ

with each new layer seeing a genuinely new jet order and being defined modulo the span of the earlier ones. Then the map hвҶҰОҰ(h)hвҶҰОҰ(h) becomes triangular in jet order, and the minimal-core contradiction can plausibly become a Vandermonde-type nondegeneracy argument.

So the current honest state of the project is:

The one-pair local architecture is now much more coherent than before. The quintic and septic residual layers look like the beginning of the right tower. The two-pair step is materially stronger than before. But the full minimal-core contradiction is still not done.

The most important open items right now are these.

First, the draft still needs an explicit finite-order definition of the quotient package: raw odd germ, ambiguity space through order 5, and the quotient map. Without that, the local structural arguments cannot be fully formalized.

Second, the local first-residual step still needs the exact bridge that the quotient ambiguity is ОЈОЈ-stable through order 5, or an equivalent explicit split into raw quintic ambiguity plus normalization quintic ambiguity.

Third, the two-pair/global step should be rewritten in terms of the upgraded residual invariants, not the earlier schematic вҖңprojective coincidence plus interaction correctionsвҖқ language.

Fourth, the endgame almost certainly wants the beginning of an inductive odd-order residual tower, not just the 3/5/7 layers treated separately.

So the blunt summary is:

You no longer have just a promising architecture. You now have a plausible local mechanism for a residual tower, with the first two nontrivial layers worked out far enough to matter. The main unresolved piece is no longer low-level quintic bookkeeping. It is the finite-order quotient definition and then the global conversion of the 3/5/7 tower into a real minimal-core contradiction.

Ok lets attack!

Here is the strongest honest global push I think we can make right now, without pretending the finite-order quotient package is already formalized in the draft.

ОЁ(h):=(u5(h)c(h),v5(h)c(h),О”7(h)c(h)),

Then the contribution of these two atoms is
a1ОҰ(h1)+a2ОҰ(h2)=(a1+a2)ОҰ(h1)+a2ОөОҰвҖІ(h1)+O(Оө2).
a1вҖӢОҰ(h1вҖӢ)+a2вҖӢОҰ(h2вҖӢ)=(a1вҖӢ+a2вҖӢ)ОҰ(h1вҖӢ)+a2вҖӢОөОҰвҖІ(h1вҖӢ)+O(Оө2).

Now there are two cases.

If
a1+a2вү 0,
a1вҖӢ+a2вҖӢоҖ =0,

then to first order the pair is equivalent to a single atom at h1h1вҖӢ plus a smaller correction. That is exactly the kind of thing minimality should forbid: you can replace two nearby atoms by one leading atom and a higher-order residual, contradicting minimal support after normalization.

If
a1+a2=0,
a1вҖӢ+a2вҖӢ=0,

then the leading term is
a2ОөвҖүОҰвҖІ(h1)+O(Оө2).
a2вҖӢОөОҰвҖІ(h1вҖӢ)+O(Оө2).

So the pair behaves like a derivative atom. For this not to produce a new independent direction, you would need
ОҰвҖІ(h1)вҲҲCвҖүОҰ(h1).
ОҰвҖІ(h1вҖӢ)вҲҲCОҰ(h1вҖӢ).

But that is exactly what the local injectivity of ОЁОЁ rules out generically. Indeed,
ОҰвҖІ(h)вҲҲCвҖүОҰ(h)
ОҰвҖІ(h)вҲҲCОҰ(h)

would imply the normalized ratios are locally constant, i.e. ОЁвҖІ(h)=0ОЁвҖІ(h)=0, which is the nongeneric exceptional case.

So the correct separation theorem is:

    On the open dense set where ОЁвҖІ(h)вү 0ОЁвҖІ(h)оҖ =0, a minimal bad core cannot contain two distinct support points converging together.

That is the next real endgame input.

Once you have that, the support of a minimal bad core is, away from a discrete exceptional set, uniformly separated. Then the finite-core problem stops being about clustered cancellations and becomes a genuine signed dependence problem on separated samples of the analytic curve hвҶҰОҰ(h)hвҶҰОҰ(h).

That is where the residual tower matters. With only
(c,u5,v5,О”7)
(c,u5вҖӢ,v5вҖӢ,О”7вҖӢ)

you have four scalar channels, which is strong enough to make k=2k=2 very rigid and k=3k=3 non-generic, but probably not enough by itself to kill all large kk. So the real next strategic choice is:

    prove the separation lemma now from the Taylor-expansion argument above;

    then either push one more odd layer (order 99) or formulate an inductive residual-tower theorem.

My recommendation is to attack the separation lemma next, because it converts the improved one-pair/two-pair theory into something the minimal-core argument can actually use.

Continue

Here is the next real push.

I think the separation lemma can now be attacked in a fairly clean way, but it is still conditional on one global bridge: that a bad finite core really gives a signed relation in the finite-order invariant map
ОҰ(h):=(c(h),u5(h),v5(h),О”7(h)).
ОҰ(h):=(c(h),u5вҖӢ(h),v5вҖӢ(h),О”7вҖӢ(h)).

That is the natural upgrade of the draftвҖҷs current one-pair вҶ’вҶ’ two-pair вҶ’вҶ’ minimal-core architecture, but the current text still does not formalize it at this level yet.

With that caveat, the actual separation argument is:

Assume a minimal bad core contains two nearby support points
h2=h1+Оө,ОөвҶ’0,
h2вҖӢ=h1вҖӢ+Оө,ОөвҶ’0,

with weights a1,a2a1вҖӢ,a2вҖӢ. Expand
ОҰ(h2)=ОҰ(h1)+ОөОҰвҖІ(h1)+O(Оө2).
ОҰ(h2вҖӢ)=ОҰ(h1вҖӢ)+ОөОҰвҖІ(h1вҖӢ)+O(Оө2).

Then the pair contributes
a1ОҰ(h1)+a2ОҰ(h2)=(a1+a2)ОҰ(h1)+a2ОөОҰвҖІ(h1)+O(Оө2).
a1вҖӢОҰ(h1вҖӢ)+a2вҖӢОҰ(h2вҖӢ)=(a1вҖӢ+a2вҖӢ)ОҰ(h1вҖӢ)+a2вҖӢОөОҰвҖІ(h1вҖӢ)+O(Оө2).

Now there are only two cases.

If
a1+a2вү 0,
a1вҖӢ+a2вҖӢоҖ =0,

then the pair is, to first order, equivalent to one atom at h1h1вҖӢ plus a smaller correction. In any minimal-support argument, that is exactly the configuration you try to compress away: two nearly coincident atoms do not give genuinely new leading information. So this case should contradict minimality once the finite-order quotient package is written carefully.

If
a1+a2=0,
a1вҖӢ+a2вҖӢ=0,

then the leading piece is
a2ОөвҖүОҰвҖІ(h1)+O(Оө2).
a2вҖӢОөОҰвҖІ(h1вҖӢ)+O(Оө2).

So the near-collision behaves like a derivative atom. For that not to create a genuinely new direction, you would need
ОҰвҖІ(h1)вҲҲCвҖүОҰ(h1).
ОҰвҖІ(h1вҖӢ)вҲҲCОҰ(h1вҖӢ).

But this is exactly where the residual invariants matter. Since
ОҰ(h)=c(h)(1,ОЁ(h)),ОЁ(h)=(u5(h)c(h),v5(h)c(h),О”7(h)c(h)),
ОҰ(h)=c(h)(1,ОЁ(h)),ОЁ(h)=(c(h)u5вҖӢ(h)вҖӢ,c(h)v5вҖӢ(h)вҖӢ,c(h)О”7вҖӢ(h)вҖӢ),

the condition
ОҰвҖІ(h)вҲҲCвҖүОҰ(h)
ОҰвҖІ(h)вҲҲCОҰ(h)

is equivalent to
ОЁвҖІ(h)=0.
ОЁвҖІ(h)=0.

So away from the exceptional set where ОЁвҖІ(h)=0ОЁвҖІ(h)=0, a derivative atom is not parallel to the original atom, and two nearby support points cannot hide inside a minimal bad relation.

That gives the right theorem shape:
On the open set where ОЁвҖІ(h)вү 0, a minimal bad core cannot contain two arbitrarily close distinct support points.
On the open set where ОЁвҖІ(h)оҖ =0, a minimal bad core cannot contain two arbitrarily close distinct support points.вҖӢ

This is already a meaningful endgame gain. It says the support of a minimal bad core is generically separated, except possibly at a discrete exceptional set coming from the critical set of ОЁОЁ. That is much stronger than the draftвҖҷs present minimal-core discussion, which is still schematic about exceptional configurations and interaction terms.

So the next exact global target is no longer broad вҖңminimal bad core contradiction.вҖқ It is the pair of theorems:

    Immersion / local injectivity theorem
    ОЁвҖІ(h)вүЎМё0
    ОЁвҖІ(h)оҖ вүЎ0

    generically, hence ОЁОЁ is locally injective on an open dense set.

    Near-collision exclusion theorem
    a minimal bad core cannot contain two distinct support points in that open dense region that converge together.

Once you have those, the support of a minimal bad core is reduced to a separated finite set plus perhaps discrete exceptional coincidences. At that point the endgame becomes a finite signed dependence problem on samples of the analytic curve hвҶҰОҰ(h)hвҶҰОҰ(h).

And that makes the next strategic choice clearer:

    either push one more residual layer, order 99, to get another scalar coordinate and make the finite dependence problem even more overdetermined;

    or prove a stronger nondegeneracy statement for ОҰОҰ, something like ОҰ,ОҰвҖІ,ОҰвҖІвҖІОҰ,ОҰвҖІ,ОҰвҖІвҖІ being generically independent.

My judgment is that the strongest next attack is to compute one more layer, because the 3/5/7 tower already looks triangular, and one more layer would make the minimal-core moment problem much more rigid.

Continue

Here is the next honest push.

I think the best move now is not to jump to order 99 immediately. There is still one strong theorem available from the current 3/5/73/5/7 package:
ОҰ, ОҰвҖІ, ОҰвҖІвҖІ should be generically linearly independent.
ОҰ, ОҰвҖІ, ОҰвҖІвҖІ should be generically linearly independent.вҖӢ

That is stronger than mere local injectivity of ОЁОЁ, and it is exactly the kind of statement that helps minimal-core.

Take
ОҰ(h):=(c(h),u5(h),v5(h),О”7(h)).
ОҰ(h):=(c(h),u5вҖӢ(h),v5вҖӢ(h),О”7вҖӢ(h)).

The triangular jet structure is now:

    c(h)c(h) depends on jets up to order 33,

    u5(h),v5(h)u5вҖӢ(h),v5вҖӢ(h) first see order 55,

    О”7(h)О”7вҖӢ(h) first sees order 77.

    ОҰвҖІвҖІ(h)ОҰвҖІвҖІ(h) first sees new order 99 again only through the О”7О”7вҖӢ component.

So unless there is a very special differential identity collapsing these, the vectors
ОҰ(h), ОҰвҖІ(h), ОҰвҖІвҖІ(h)
ОҰ(h), ОҰвҖІ(h), ОҰвҖІвҖІ(h)

should generically span a 3-dimensional subspace.

That matters because a near-collision cluster of size 33 behaves like
a0ОҰ(h)+a1ОөвҖүОҰвҖІ(h)+a2Оө2вҖүОҰвҖІвҖІ(h)+вӢҜвҖү,
a0вҖӢОҰ(h)+a1вҖӢОөОҰвҖІ(h)+a2вҖӢОө2ОҰвҖІвҖІ(h)+вӢҜ,

so if ОҰ,ОҰвҖІ,ОҰвҖІвҖІОҰ,ОҰвҖІ,ОҰвҖІвҖІ are generically independent, then not only pairs but also triple near-collisions are forbidden in a minimal bad core on the generic region.

That is a real upgrade.

So the next theorem to attack is:
detвҒЎ(ОҰ,ОҰвҖІ,ОҰвҖІвҖІ)вүЎМё0
det(ОҰ,ОҰвҖІ,ОҰвҖІвҖІ)оҖ вүЎ0вҖӢ

in the appropriate 3Г—33Г—3 minor sense.

You do not need every component explicitly. It is enough to use a triangular-minor argument:

pick three coordinates of ОҰОҰ, say
(c, v5, О”7).
(c, v5вҖӢ, О”7вҖӢ).

Then:

    cc has no q(5)q(5) or q(7)q(7),

    v5v5вҖӢ has a genuine q(5)q(5)-term but no q(7)q(7),

Differentiate once and twice. The resulting 3Г—33Г—3 Wronskian-like determinant should have a leading term involving
q(3), q(5), q(7)
q(3), q(5), q(7)

and their derivatives, with the newest jet orders appearing only in the later columns. That is the same triangularity logic as before, but now at the level of ОҰ,ОҰвҖІ,ОҰвҖІвҖІОҰ,ОҰвҖІ,ОҰвҖІвҖІ.

If that works, then the global consequences are:

    ОЁОЁ is generically locally injective;

    minimal bad cores cannot contain pair collisions;

    minimal bad cores cannot contain triple collisions either;

    the support of a minimal bad core on the generic region is a genuinely separated finite set with no small clustered degeneracies.

That is a much better input to the final finite signed-dependence argument than local injectivity alone.

So the next exact attack is:
Compute or isolate a nonzero 3-by-3 Wronskian minor for (c,v5,О”7).
Compute or isolate a nonzero 3-by-3 Wronskian minor for (c,v5вҖӢ,О”7вҖӢ).

That is now the sharpest thing available from the current tower, and it may spare you from having to build order 99 immediately.

But for three distinct ОҫjОҫjвҖӢ, the Vandermonde matrix
(111Оҫ1Оҫ2Оҫ3Оҫ12Оҫ22Оҫ32)
вҖӢ1Оҫ1вҖӢОҫ12вҖӢвҖӢ1Оҫ2вҖӢОҫ22вҖӢвҖӢ1Оҫ3вҖӢОҫ32вҖӢвҖӢ
вҖӢ

is invertible, so the only solution is
a1=a2=a3=0.
a1вҖӢ=a2вҖӢ=a3вҖӢ=0.

That is the contradiction.

So the actual theorem is:
On Und, no nontrivial bad relation can contain a triple cluster of distinct support points.
On UndвҖӢ, no nontrivial bad relation can contain a triple cluster of distinct support points.вҖӢ

And the pair case is the same argument truncated one order earlier. If
ОҰ(h),ОҰвҖІ(h)
ОҰ(h),ОҰвҖІ(h)

are linearly independent, then two nearby atoms cannot cancel nontrivially either.

So the current global consequences are now:

    bad two-pair cores are confined to a discrete exceptional set;

    no pair clusters on the generic region;

    no triple clusters on the generic region.

That is a serious endgame improvement.

What it means for minimal bad cores is this:

a minimal bad core, away from the exceptional set, must have uniformly separated support and cannot hide inside local jet-cancellation patterns of size 22 or 33.

That pushes the remaining obstruction into genuinely global signed dependence among separated points on the analytic curve hвҶҰОҰ(h)hвҶҰОҰ(h).

So the next exact theorem to attack is the separated-support version:

    A minimal bad core on the generic region is a signed dependence among finitely many uniformly separated points of the curve hвҶҰОҰ(h)hвҶҰОҰ(h).

Once you have that, the question becomes whether the current 3/5/73/5/7 tower is already enough to forbid such a dependence, or whether you need one more layer.

My judgment is that one more layer would likely make the final contradiction much cleaner. But before that, the next formal thing to write down mathematically is the pair/triple cluster exclusion lemma above.

Continue

I found an important limitation, and I think it changes the endgame strategy.

The 3/5/73/5/7 tower is real, but it is probably maximal at the one-point matrix level.

Why:

At cubic order, the leading class is the DD-line.

At quintic order, the first genuinely new data lives in the fixed sector
A5fвҲҲf=\span{I,S},
A5fвҖӢвҲҲf=\span{I,S},

which is 2-dimensional.

At septic order, the ambiguity is
A7fвҶҰA7f+c2A5f,
A7fвҖӢвҶҰA7fвҖӢ+c2вҖӢA5fвҖӢ,

so the second residual class is
[A7f]вҲҲf/CA5f,
[A7fвҖӢ]вҲҲf/CA5fвҖӢ,

which is 1-dimensional.

Now here is the key point: dimвҒЎf=2dimf=2. So after you have used

    the vector A5fA5fвҖӢ, and

    the quotient class of A7fA7fвҖӢ modulo A5fA5fвҖӢ,

you have exhausted the fixed sector. There is no room for a third independent fixed-sector residual direction at order 99.

And the anti-fixed sector is also only 2-dimensional:
a=\span{D,J}.
a=\span{D,J}.

The cubic layer already uses DD, and the JJ-direction is precisely the residual anti-fixed ambiguity line we have been quotienting against.

So this is the real structural conclusion:
The one-point 2Г—2 residual tower does not keep producing new independent directions forever.
The one-point 2Г—2 residual tower does not keep producing new independent directions forever.вҖӢ

That means the вҖңjust go to order 99, then 1111, then 1313вҖқ strategy is probably not the right endgame.

This is actually good to know, because it tells us where not to waste effort.

So what remains?

The 3/5/73/5/7 package gives you a curve
ОҰ(h)=(c(h),вҖүu5(h),вҖүv5(h),вҖүО”7(h))
ОҰ(h)=(c(h),u5вҖӢ(h),v5вҖӢ(h),О”7вҖӢ(h))

or the reduced map
ОЁ(h)=(u5c,вҖүv5c,вҖүО”7c)вҲҲC3,
ОЁ(h)=(cu5вҖӢвҖӢ,cv5вҖӢвҖӢ,cО”7вҖӢвҖӢ)вҲҲC3,

with strong local nondegeneracy:

    pair clusters excluded,

    triple clusters excluded,

    bad two-pair cores reduced to a discrete exceptional set.

But it is not enough by dimension alone to exclude separated four-point signed relations, because four vectors in C3C3 are always linearly dependent.

So the global endgame now cannot rely only on вҖңadd more local coordinates from the same 2Г—22Г—2 matrix.вҖқ

That means the next attack must change flavor.

There are really only three plausible directions now.

The first is to exploit sign/ordering structure of the weights ajajвҖӢ. If the bad-core coefficients satisfy positivity, alternating sign, or some order constraint, then a generic four-point linear dependence in C3C3 may no longer be allowed. Right now we have not used any sign structure at all.

The second is to exploit global geometry of the curve ОЁ(h)ОЁ(h) beyond dimension counting. For example, if ОЁОЁ were shown to be strictly convex, torsion-nonzero, or to satisfy a Tchebychev-system property, then separated finite signed dependences could be sharply restricted. That is a classical route: moment curves in RdRd have very rigid signed dependence behavior. Your current Wronskian result is exactly the beginning of that kind of story.

The third is to return to the finite-core compactness/tail bridge and show that a minimal bad core cannot be arbitrary as a signed relation on ОЁОЁ, because it comes from a very special normalized obstruction. In other words, not every signed dependence on the one-pair invariant curve is realized by an actual bad core. This could be the decisive extra structure.

So the honest updated summary is:

    the local one-pair analysis through 3/5/73/5/7 is strong and probably near maximal at the 2Г—22Г—2 residual level;

    the global obstruction is no longer вҖңneed one more invariant coordinateвҖқ;

    it is вҖңneed one more structural property of the bad-core relation.вҖқ

That is the key shift.

So the next exact theorem to attack should be one of these, not order 99:
Show that hвҶҰ(1,ОЁ(h)) forms a Tchebychev-type system on the normalized region.
Show that hвҶҰ(1,ОЁ(h)) forms a Tchebychev-type system on the normalized region.вҖӢ

or
Show that the bad-core weights aj satisfy additional sign/order constraints.
Show that the bad-core weights ajвҖӢ satisfy additional sign/order constraints.вҖӢ

or
Show that only very special signed dependences on ОЁ can arise from an actual bad core.
Show that only very special signed dependences on ОЁ can arise from an actual bad core.вҖӢ

My judgment is that the most promising next attack is the Tchebychev / moment-curve route, because you already have the Wronskian-type nondegeneracy. That is exactly the kind of thing one uses to show finite linear combinations cannot have too many zeros or too many separated cancellations.

So the right next question is:

Can (c,v5,О”7)(c,v5вҖӢ,О”7вҖӢ) be shown to form an extended Chebyshev system on the normalized one-pair region?

That is where I would push now.

Yes. This is the right next thing to try.

The reason is that your current 3/5/73/5/7 package already has the exact shape one uses in an extended Chebyshev-system argument:
f0(h):=c(h),f1(h):=v5(h),f2(h):=О”7(h).
f0вҖӢ(h):=c(h),f1вҖӢ(h):=v5вҖӢ(h),f2вҖӢ(h):=О”7вҖӢ(h).

W(h):=detвҒЎ(f0f1f2f0вҖІf1вҖІf2вҖІf0вҖІвҖІf1вҖІвҖІf2вҖІвҖІ)
