\subsection{Fixed-sector refinement at quintic and septic order}

The trace witness at quintic order suggests a more structured refinement of the
one-pair residual germ than the trace alone records. For bookkeeping purposes,
it is convenient to decompose the quintic and septic coefficients into fixed and
anti-fixed parts with respect to the involution
\[
\iota(A):=J_0AJ_0,
\qquad
J_0=
\begin{pmatrix}
0&1\\
1&0
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Write
\[
\pi_{\mathfrak f}(A):=\frac12(A+J_0AJ_0),
\qquad
\pi_{\mathfrak a}(A):=\frac12(A-J_0AJ_0).
\]
Then
\[
\mathfrak f=\operatorname{span}\{I,S\},
\qquad
\mathfrak a=\operatorname{span}\{D,J\},
\]
where
\[
I=
\begin{pmatrix}
1&0\\0&1
\end{pmatrix},
\quad
S=
\begin{pmatrix}
0&1\\1&0
\end{pmatrix},
\quad
D=
\begin{pmatrix}
1&0\\0&-1
\end{pmatrix},
\quad
J=
\begin{pmatrix}
0&-1\\1&0
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

\begin{definition}[Fixed-sector quintic and septic coefficients]
\label{def:fixed-sector-quintic-septic}
Let
\[
F_h(z)=A_3(h)z^3+A_5(h)z^5+A_7(h)z^7+\cdots
\]
denote the normalized one-pair quotient odd germ on the cubic-generic region.
Its fixed-sector quintic and septic coefficients are defined by
\[
A_5^{\mathfrak f}(h):=\pi_{\mathfrak f}(A_5(h)),
\qquad
A_7^{\mathfrak f}(h):=\pi_{\mathfrak f}(A_7(h)).
\]
Write
\[
A_5^{\mathfrak f}(h)=u_5(h)\,I+v_5(h)\,S,
\qquad
A_7^{\mathfrak f}(h)=u_7(h)\,I+v_7(h)\,S.
\]
\end{definition}

\begin{definition}[Second residual determinant]
\label{def:second-residual-determinant}
On the cubic-generic region, define
\[
\Delta_7(h):=u_7(h)v_5(h)-u_5(h)v_7(h).
\]
\end{definition}

\begin{remark}[Derived fixed-sector identities]
\label{rem:derived-fixed-sector-identities}
The working analysis gives the following identities, which should be proved in
a later draft in the paper's native coefficient language.

\begin{enumerate}
\item If
\[
X_1=
\begin{pmatrix}
u&v\\v&w
\end{pmatrix},
\qquad
X_3=
\begin{pmatrix}
\alpha&\beta\\\beta&\gamma
\end{pmatrix},
\]
then the quintic fixed-sector \(S\)-component coming from the commutator-type
channel is
\[
v_5=\gamma u-\alpha w.
\]

\item The direct linear \(q^{(7)}\)-driven contribution to the septic
fixed-sector coefficient is \(I\)-directional. In particular, on that slice,
\[
v_7^{(q^{(7)})}=0,
\qquad
\Delta_7^{(q^{(7)})}=u_7^{(q^{(7)})}v_5.
\]
Thus the determinant \(\Delta_7\) is the natural second residual scalar once
\(A_5^{\mathfrak f}\) has been fixed.
\end{enumerate}
\end{remark}

\begin{remark}[Work in progress: normalized affine one-pair invariant curve]
\label{rem:wip-normalized-affine-one-pair-curve}
The later working analysis suggests the normalized affine chart
\[
x(h):=\frac{v_5(h)}{c(h)},
\qquad
y(h):=\frac{\Delta_7(h)}{c(h)}
\]
on the cubic-generic region. The intended geometric program is to study the
planar curve
\[
h\longmapsto (x(h),y(h))
\]
rather than only the scalar quintic trace. In this formulation, the remaining
generic obstruction is reduced to common four-secant lines of this planar
curve.
\end{remark}

This block ensures that u5,v5,u7,v7,О”7u5вҖӢ,v5вҖӢ,u7вҖӢ,v7вҖӢ,О”7вҖӢ are actually in the draft and not just in the conversation.

\begin{remark}[Work in progress: planar convexity reformulation]
\label{rem:wip-planar-convexity-reformulation}
The later working analysis suggests that the remaining generic obstruction
should be reformulated as a planar geometry problem for the curve
\[
h\longmapsto \left(\frac{v_5(h)}{c(h)},\frac{\Delta_7(h)}{c(h)}\right).
\]
On the open set where
\[
c(h)\neq 0,
\qquad
\frac{d}{dh}\!\left(\frac{v_5(h)}{c(h)}\right)\neq 0,
\qquad
\det\!\begin{pmatrix}
x'(h) & y'(h)\\
x''(h) & y''(h)
\end{pmatrix}\neq 0,
\]
with
\[
x(h)=\frac{v_5(h)}{c(h)},
\qquad
y(h)=\frac{\Delta_7(h)}{c(h)},
\]
the image breaks into strictly convex graph components. On each such component,
a line meets the curve in at most two points. The remaining generic global
obstruction is therefore reduced to the possibility of a common four-secant
line meeting two separated convex components twice each.

In this formulation, the unresolved endgame is no longer local quintic
bookkeeping but endpoint/support-function geometry for the planar curve
\((v_5/c,\Delta_7/c)\).
\end{remark}

for some generically nonzero scalars u,au,a. Meanwhile the first-order О·2О·2вҖӢ-slice is symmetric off-diagonal:
X1(О·2)=vвҖүS.
X1(О·2вҖӢ)вҖӢ=vS.

This is all explicitly in the conversation.

X5JX1вҲ’X1JX5.

JS=вҲ’D,SJ=D,D2=I,

вҲ’2avвҖүI.

\begin{proposition}[Explicit fixed-sector formulas at quintic order]
\label{prop:explicit-quintic-fixed-sector}
Write
\[
X_1=
\begin{pmatrix}
u&v\\
v&w
\end{pmatrix},
\qquad
X_3=
\begin{pmatrix}
\alpha&\beta\\
\beta&\gamma
\end{pmatrix}.
\]
Then the fixed-sector part of the basic quintic commutator channel
\[
X_3JX_1-X_1JX_3
\]
is
\[
\pi_{\mathfrak f}(X_3JX_1-X_1JX_3)
=
\bigl(v(\gamma-\alpha)+\beta(u-w)\bigr)I
+
(\gamma u-\alpha w)S.
\]
Consequently, for the quintic fixed-sector coefficient
\[
A_5^{\mathfrak f}(h)=u_5(h)\,I+v_5(h)\,S,
\]
the commutator-type contribution is explicitly
\[
u_5^{(\mathrm{comm})}
=
v(\gamma-\alpha)+\beta(u-w),
\qquad
v_5^{(\mathrm{comm})}
=
\gamma u-\alpha w.
\]
\end{proposition}

\begin{proof}
A direct multiplication gives
\[
X_3JX_1-X_1JX_3
=
\begin{pmatrix}
-2\alpha\beta+2\beta u & \gamma u-\alpha w\\[.5ex]
\gamma u-\alpha w & -2\beta w+2\gamma\beta
\end{pmatrix},
\]
and hence its fixed-sector projection is obtained by taking the average of the
diagonal entries for the \(I\)-component and the common off-diagonal entry for
the \(S\)-component:
\[
\pi_{\mathfrak f}(X_3JX_1-X_1JX_3)
=
\frac{(-2\alpha v+2\beta u)+(-2\beta w+2\gamma v)}{2}\,I
+
(\gamma u-\alpha w)S.
\]
This simplifies to
\[
\bigl(v(\gamma-\alpha)+\beta(u-w)\bigr)I
+
(\gamma u-\alpha w)S,
\]
as claimed.
\end{proof}

\begin{proposition}[Explicit septic \(K_1\)-transport fixed-sector formulas]
\label{prop:explicit-septic-k1-fixed-sector}
Write
\[
X_0=\diag(x_1,x_2),
\qquad
X_1=
\begin{pmatrix}
u&v\\
v&w
\end{pmatrix},
\qquad
X_2=
\begin{pmatrix}
a&b\\
b&c
\end{pmatrix},
\]
\[
X_4=
\begin{pmatrix}
p&r\\
r&s
\end{pmatrix},
\qquad
X_5=
\begin{pmatrix}
\alpha_5&\beta_5\\
\beta_5&\gamma_5
\end{pmatrix},
\qquad
X_6=
\begin{pmatrix}
\mu_6&\nu_6\\
\nu_6&\lambda_6
\end{pmatrix}.
\]
Then the fixed-sector parts of the three septic \(K_1\)-transport channels are
\[
\pi_{\mathfrak f}(X_5JX_1-X_1JX_5)
=
\bigl(\beta_5(u-w)+v(\gamma_5-\alpha_5)\bigr)I
+
(\gamma_5u-\alpha_5w)S,
\]
\[
\pi_{\mathfrak f}(X_2JX_4-X_4JX_2)
=
\bigl(b(p-s)+r(c-a)\bigr)I
+
(cp-as)S,
\]
and
\[
\pi_{\mathfrak f}(X_0JX_6-X_6JX_0)
=
(x_2-x_1)\nu_6\,I
+
(x_2\mu_6-x_1\lambda_6)S.
\]

Consequently, if the septic \(K_1\)-transport fixed-sector coefficient is
written
\[
A_{7,K_1}^{\mathfrak f}(h)=u_{7,K_1}(h)\,I+v_{7,K_1}(h)\,S,
\]
then
\[
u_{7,K_1}
=
\kappa\Bigl(
(x_2-x_1)\nu_6
+
\beta_5(u-w)+v(\gamma_5-\alpha_5)
+
b(p-s)+r(c-a)
\Bigr),
\]
\[
v_{7,K_1}
=
\kappa\Bigl(
x_2\mu_6-x_1\lambda_6
+
\gamma_5u-\alpha_5w
+
cp-as
\Bigr).
\]
\end{proposition}

\begin{proof}
For two symmetric matrices
\[
X=
\begin{pmatrix}
a&b\\
b&c
\end{pmatrix},
\qquad
Y=
\begin{pmatrix}
p&q\\
q&r
\end{pmatrix},
\]
a direct calculation gives
\[
XJY-YJX
=
\begin{pmatrix}
-2aq+2bp & -ar+cp\\
-ar+cp & -2br+2cq
\end{pmatrix}.
\]
Taking fixed-sector projection yields
\[
\pi_{\mathfrak f}(XJY-YJX)
=
\bigl(b(p-r)+q(c-a)\bigr)I
+
(cp-ar)S.
\]
Applying this formula to \((X,Y)=(X_5,X_1)\) and \((X,Y)=(X_2,X_4)\) gives the
first two displayed identities.

For the \(X_0/X_6\) channel, a direct multiplication gives
\[
X_0JX_6-X_6JX_0
=
\begin{pmatrix}
-2x_1\nu_6 & -x_1\lambda_6+x_2\mu_6\\
-x_1\lambda_6+x_2\mu_6 & 2x_2\nu_6
\end{pmatrix},
\]
whose fixed-sector part is
\[
(x_2-x_1)\nu_6\,I
+
(x_2\mu_6-x_1\lambda_6)S.
\]
Summing the three channels gives the stated formulas for
\(u_{7,K_1}\) and \(v_{7,K_1}\).
\end{proof}

\begin{corollary}[Direct \(q^{(7)}\)-driven septic slice]
\label{cor:direct-q7-septic-slice}
On the direct linear \(q^{(7)}\)-driven slice, the septic \(K_1\)-transport
fixed-sector contribution is \(I\)-directional. More precisely,
\[
v_{7,K_1}^{(q^{(7)})}=0,
\]
and
\[
u_{7,K_1}^{(q^{(7)})}
=
\kappa\Bigl(
(x_2-x_1)\nu_6^{(q^{(7)})}
+
v\,(\gamma_5-\alpha_5)^{(q^{(7)})}
\Bigr),
\]
so in particular
\[
\Delta_7^{(q^{(7)})}=u_7^{(q^{(7)})}v_5.
\]
\end{corollary}

\begin{proof}
On the direct \(q^{(7)}\)-driven slice, the linear \(X_5\)-input is diagonal,
hence
\[
\beta_5^{(q^{(7)})}=0.
\]
The mixed \(X_6\)-slice is off-diagonal on this grading, so
\[
\mu_6^{(q^{(7)})}=\lambda_6^{(q^{(7)})}=0.
\]
Moreover the \(X_2/X_4\)-channel does not lie on the pure
\((K_1,\eta_2,q^{(7)})\)-slice. Therefore the \(S\)-component vanishes and the
displayed formula for \(u_{7,K_1}^{(q^{(7)})}\) follows from
Proposition~\ref{prop:explicit-septic-k1-fixed-sector}. Since
\[
\Delta_7=u_7v_5-u_5v_7,
\]
the direct \(q^{(7)}\)-slice satisfies
\[
\Delta_7^{(q^{(7)})}=u_7^{(q^{(7)})}v_5.
\qedhere
\]
\end{proof}

\begin{remark}[Status of explicit fixed-sector formulas]
\label{rem:status-explicit-fixed-sector-formulas}
Proposition~\ref{prop:explicit-quintic-fixed-sector} gives an explicit
coefficient formula for the quintic commutator contribution to
\(A_5^{\mathfrak f}\), and
Proposition~\ref{prop:explicit-septic-k1-fixed-sector} gives an explicit
formula for the septic \(K_1\)-transport contribution to
\(A_7^{\mathfrak f}\). What is still not known in full explicit form is the
total septic fixed-sector coefficient after all order-7 sectors are included.
Thus the formulas above should be read as the explicit transport-level
contribution underlying the second residual determinant \(\Delta_7\), not yet
as a closed formula for the full total pair \((u_7,v_7)\).
\end{remark}

\begin{proposition}[Explicit fixed-sector formulas at quintic order]
\label{prop:explicit-quintic-fixed-sector}
Write
\[
X_1=
\begin{pmatrix}
u&v\\
v&w
\end{pmatrix},
\qquad
X_3=
\begin{pmatrix}
\alpha&\beta\\
\beta&\gamma
\end{pmatrix}.
\]
Then the fixed-sector part of the basic quintic commutator channel
\[
X_3JX_1-X_1JX_3
\]
is
\[
\pi_{\mathfrak f}(X_3JX_1-X_1JX_3)
=
\bigl(v(\gamma-\alpha)+\beta(u-w)\bigr)I
+
(\gamma u-\alpha w)S.
\]
Consequently, for the quintic fixed-sector coefficient
\[
A_5^{\mathfrak f}(h)=u_5(h)\,I+v_5(h)\,S,
\]
the commutator-type contribution is explicitly
\[
u_5^{(\mathrm{comm})}
=
v(\gamma-\alpha)+\beta(u-w),
\qquad
v_5^{(\mathrm{comm})}
=
\gamma u-\alpha w.
\]
\end{proposition}

\begin{proof}
A direct computation gives
\[
X_3JX_1-X_1JX_3
=
\begin{pmatrix}
-2\alpha v+2\beta u & -\alpha w+\gamma u\\[.5ex]
-\alpha w+\gamma u & -2\beta w+2\gamma v
\end{pmatrix}.
\]
The fixed-sector projection of a symmetric matrix
\[
\begin{pmatrix}
A&B\\
B&C
\end{pmatrix}
\]
is
\[
\pi_{\mathfrak f}
\begin{pmatrix}
A&B\\
B&C
\end{pmatrix}
=
\frac{A+C}{2}\,I+B\,S.
\]
Applying this to the displayed matrix gives
\[
\pi_{\mathfrak f}(X_3JX_1-X_1JX_3)
=
\frac{(-2\alpha v+2\beta u)+(-2\beta w+2\gamma v)}{2}\,I
+
(\gamma u-\alpha w)S,
\]
which simplifies to
\[
\bigl(v(\gamma-\alpha)+\beta(u-w)\bigr)I
+
(\gamma u-\alpha w)S.
\]
\end{proof}

\begin{proposition}[Explicit septic \(K_1\)-transport fixed-sector formulas]
\label{prop:explicit-septic-k1-fixed-sector}
Write
\[
X_0=\diag(x_1,x_2),
\qquad
X_1=
\begin{pmatrix}
u&v\\
v&w
\end{pmatrix},
\qquad
X_2=
\begin{pmatrix}
a&b\\
b&c
\end{pmatrix},
\]
\[
X_4=
\begin{pmatrix}
p&r\\
r&s
\end{pmatrix},
\qquad
X_5=
\begin{pmatrix}
\alpha_5&\beta_5\\
\beta_5&\gamma_5
\end{pmatrix},
\qquad
X_6=
\begin{pmatrix}
\mu_6&\nu_6\\
\nu_6&\lambda_6
\end{pmatrix}.
\]
Then the fixed-sector parts of the three septic \(K_1\)-transport channels are
\[
\pi_{\mathfrak f}(X_5JX_1-X_1JX_5)
=
\bigl(\beta_5(u-w)+v(\gamma_5-\alpha_5)\bigr)I
+
(\gamma_5u-\alpha_5w)S,
\]
\[
\pi_{\mathfrak f}(X_2JX_4-X_4JX_2)
=
\bigl(b(p-s)+r(c-a)\bigr)I
+
(cp-as)S,
\]
and
\[
\pi_{\mathfrak f}(X_0JX_6-X_6JX_0)
=
(x_2-x_1)\nu_6\,I
+
(x_2\mu_6-x_1\lambda_6)S.
\]

Consequently, if the septic \(K_1\)-transport fixed-sector coefficient is
written
\[
A_{7,K_1}^{\mathfrak f}(h)=u_{7,K_1}(h)\,I+v_{7,K_1}(h)\,S,
\]
then
\[
u_{7,K_1}
=
\kappa\Bigl(
(x_2-x_1)\nu_6
+
\beta_5(u-w)+v(\gamma_5-\alpha_5)
+
b(p-s)+r(c-a)
\Bigr),
\]
\[
v_{7,K_1}
=
\kappa\Bigl(
x_2\mu_6-x_1\lambda_6
+
\gamma_5u-\alpha_5w
+
cp-as
\Bigr).
\]
\end{proposition}

\begin{proof}
For symmetric matrices
\[
X=
\begin{pmatrix}
a&b\\
b&c
\end{pmatrix},
\qquad
Y=
\begin{pmatrix}
p&q\\
q&r
\end{pmatrix},
\]
a direct computation gives
\[
XJY-YJX
=
\begin{pmatrix}
-2aq+2bp & -ar+cp\\
-ar+cp & -2br+2cq
\end{pmatrix}.
\]
Projecting to the fixed sector yields
\[
\pi_{\mathfrak f}(XJY-YJX)
=
\bigl(b(p-r)+q(c-a)\bigr)I
+
(cp-ar)S.
\]
Applying this formula to \((X,Y)=(X_5,X_1)\) and \((X,Y)=(X_2,X_4)\) gives the
first two displayed identities.

For the \(X_0/X_6\) channel, one computes directly
\[
X_0JX_6-X_6JX_0
=
\begin{pmatrix}
-2x_1\nu_6 & -x_1\lambda_6+x_2\mu_6\\
-x_1\lambda_6+x_2\mu_6 & 2x_2\nu_6
\end{pmatrix},
\]
whose fixed-sector projection is
\[
(x_2-x_1)\nu_6\,I
+
(x_2\mu_6-x_1\lambda_6)S.
\]
Summing the three fixed-sector contributions gives the stated formulas for
\(u_{7,K_1}\) and \(v_{7,K_1}\).
\end{proof}

\begin{corollary}[Direct \(q^{(7)}\)-driven septic slice]
\label{cor:direct-q7-septic-slice}
On the direct linear \(q^{(7)}\)-driven slice, the septic \(K_1\)-transport
fixed-sector contribution is \(I\)-directional. More precisely,
\[
v_{7,K_1}^{(q^{(7)})}=0,
\]
and
\[
u_{7,K_1}^{(q^{(7)})}
=
\kappa\Bigl(
(x_2-x_1)\nu_6^{(q^{(7)})}
+
v\,(\gamma_5-\alpha_5)^{(q^{(7)})}
\Bigr).
\]
In particular,
\[
\Delta_7^{(q^{(7)})}=u_7^{(q^{(7)})}v_5.
\]
\end{corollary}

\begin{proof}
On the direct \(q^{(7)}\)-driven slice, the linear \(X_5\)-input is diagonal,
so
\[
\beta_5^{(q^{(7)})}=0.
\]
The mixed \(X_6\)-slice is off-diagonal on this grading, so
\[
\mu_6^{(q^{(7)})}=\lambda_6^{(q^{(7)})}=0.
\]
Moreover the \(X_2/X_4\)-channel does not lie on the pure
\((K_1,\eta_2,q^{(7)})\)-slice. Therefore the \(S\)-component vanishes and the
displayed formula for \(u_{7,K_1}^{(q^{(7)})}\) follows from
Proposition~\ref{prop:explicit-septic-k1-fixed-sector}. Since
\[
\Delta_7=u_7v_5-u_5v_7,
\]
the direct \(q^{(7)}\)-slice satisfies
\[
\Delta_7^{(q^{(7)})}=u_7^{(q^{(7)})}v_5.
\qedhere
\]
\end{proof}

\begin{remark}[Status of explicit fixed-sector formulas]
\label{rem:status-explicit-fixed-sector-formulas}
Proposition~\ref{prop:explicit-quintic-fixed-sector} gives an explicit
coefficient formula for the quintic commutator contribution to
\(A_5^{\mathfrak f}\), and
Proposition~\ref{prop:explicit-septic-k1-fixed-sector} gives an explicit
formula for the septic \(K_1\)-transport contribution to
\(A_7^{\mathfrak f}\). What is still not known in full explicit form is the
total septic fixed-sector coefficient after all order-\(7\) sectors are
included. Thus the formulas above should be read as the explicit
transport-level contribution underlying the second residual determinant
\(\Delta_7\), not yet as a closed formula for the full total pair
\((u_7,v_7)\).
\end{remark}

That should now be softened, because with the block above, order 5 and the septic K1K1вҖӢ-transport sector are explicitly translated.

incomplete is the full closed coefficient formula for the total septic
fixed-sector pair \((u_7,v_7)\) after all order-\(7\) sectors are included.
These are nevertheless the natural variables for the geometric reformulation
of the multi-pair problem.

That is the cleanest way to get the most recent derivation into the paper exactly where it belongs.

The one important correction from the later conversation is this:

the right order-7 scalar is not raw septic trace.
Septic trace is not gauge-invariant. The correct second residual scalar is the fixed-sector quotient scalar, which in coordinates is exactly О”7О”7вҖӢ. Equivalently, if
A5f=u5I+v5S,
A5fвҖӢ=u5вҖӢI+v5вҖӢS,

then the unique fixed-sector linear functional annihilating A5fA5fвҖӢ is represented by
Th:=v5(h)вҖүIвҲ’u5(h)вҖүS,
ThвҖӢ:=v5вҖӢ(h)IвҲ’u5вҖӢ(h)S,

since under the trace pairing on f=\span{I,S}f=\span{I,S},
trвҒЎ(ThA5f)=0.
tr(ThвҖӢA5fвҖӢ)=0.

Then
Пғ(h):=trвҒЎ(ThA7f(h))=2(v5u7вҲ’u5v7)=2О”7(h).
Пғ(h):=tr(ThвҖӢA7fвҖӢ(h))=2(v5вҖӢu7вҖӢвҲ’u5вҖӢv7вҖӢ)=2О”7вҖӢ(h).

So О”7О”7вҖӢ is the canonical second residual scalar.

That means the remaining local gap is no longer вҖңwhat is the second invariant?вҖқ We now know the right one. The remaining local question is narrower:
prove that О”7(h)вүЎМё0 generically on the cubic/quintic generic region.
prove that О”7вҖӢ(h)оҖ вүЎ0 generically on the cubic/quintic generic region.вҖӢ

And we can push that quite far already.

From the septic derivation we now have:

    the direct q(7)q(7)-driven slice of A7fA7fвҖӢ is II-directional,

    hence
    v7(q(7))=0,
    v7(q(7))вҖӢ=0,

    and therefore
    О”7(q(7))=u7(q(7))v5.
    О”7(q(7))вҖӢ=u7(q(7))вҖӢv5вҖӢ.

So if the direct q(7)q(7)-coefficient in the II-direction is generically nonzero, and v5вүЎМё0v5вҖӢоҖ вүЎ0, then О”7вүЎМё0О”7вҖӢоҖ вүЎ0. That is the exact septic analogue of the quintic witness logic.

Define the normalized scalars

ПҒ2 is genuinely new, i.e. it is not an affine function of ПҒ1.
ПҒ2вҖӢ is genuinely new, i.e. it is not an affine function of ПҒ1вҖӢ.вҖӢ

would force the direct q(7)q(7)-coefficient of ПҒ2ПҒ2вҖӢ to vanish, contradicting the septic witness once established. This is exactly the triangularity argument the conversation was already using for the two-pair coincidence map.

G(h1,h2):=(ПҒ1(h1)вҲ’ПҒ1(h2), ПҒ2(h1)вҲ’ПҒ2(h2)).

О”7=Av5+Bc
О”7вҖӢ=Av5вҖӢ+Bc

would force the direct q(7)q(7)-coefficient of the left-hand side to vanish, contradiction. Dividing by cc gives the displayed non-affinity of ПҒ2ПҒ2вҖӢ.

That already gives a real global gain: the curve
О“(h):=(ПҒ1(h),ПҒ2(h))
О“(h):=(ПҒ1вҖӢ(h),ПҒ2вҖӢ(h))

is not contained in an affine line.

Now define the secant-coincidence map
ОЈ(h1,h2):=О“(h2)вҲ’О“(h1)=(ПҒ1(h2)вҲ’ПҒ1(h1), ПҒ2(h2)вҲ’ПҒ2(h1)).
ОЈ(h1вҖӢ,h2вҖӢ):=О“(h2вҖӢ)вҲ’О“(h1вҖӢ)=(ПҒ1вҖӢ(h2вҖӢ)вҲ’ПҒ1вҖӢ(h1вҖӢ), ПҒ2вҖӢ(h2вҖӢ)вҲ’ПҒ2вҖӢ(h1вҖӢ)).

A two-pair coincidence for the strengthened invariant data is exactly a projective equality of such secants:
[ОЈ(h1,h2)]=[ОЈ(k1,k2)].
[ОЈ(h1вҖӢ,h2вҖӢ)]=[ОЈ(k1вҖӢ,k2вҖӢ)].

So the real local-to-global derivative object is
D(h1,h2):=detвҒЎвҖүвҒЈ(ПҒ1вҖІ(h1)ПҒ1вҖІ(h2)ПҒ1(h2)вҲ’ПҒ1(h1)ПҒ2вҖІ(h1)ПҒ2вҖІ(h2)ПҒ2(h2)вҲ’ПҒ2(h1)),
D(h1вҖӢ,h2вҖӢ):=det(ПҒ1вҖІвҖӢ(h1вҖӢ)ПҒ2вҖІвҖӢ(h1вҖӢ)вҖӢПҒ1вҖІвҖӢ(h2вҖӢ)ПҒ2вҖІвҖӢ(h2вҖӢ)вҖӢПҒ1вҖӢ(h2вҖӢ)вҲ’ПҒ1вҖӢ(h1вҖӢ)ПҒ2вҖӢ(h2вҖӢ)вҲ’ПҒ2вҖӢ(h1вҖӢ)вҖӢ),

which in planar language is the condition for the secant-direction map to be immersive. More transparently, near the diagonal h2=h1+Оөh2вҖӢ=h1вҖӢ+Оө, immersion is controlled by the curvature numerator
KО“(h):=ПҒ1вҖІ(h)ПҒ2вҖІвҖІ(h)вҲ’ПҒ2вҖІ(h)ПҒ1вҖІвҖІ(h).
KО“вҖӢ(h):=ПҒ1вҖІвҖӢ(h)ПҒ2вҖІвҖІвҖӢ(h)вҲ’ПҒ2вҖІвҖӢ(h)ПҒ1вҖІвҖІвҖӢ(h).

So the next exact target is:
KО“вүЎМё0.
KО“вҖӢоҖ вүЎ0.вҖӢ

If that holds, then on an open dense set the curve О“О“ breaks into strictly convex graph components. On each such component:

    tangent slope is strictly monotone;

    a line meets the curve in at most two points;

    and the remaining bad-core obstruction reduces to a common 4-secant line meeting two separated convex components twice each.

That is exactly the secant/tangent program we had before вҖ” but now it is finally attached to the correct second scalar.

So the attack now splits cleanly into two subproblems.
1. Prove nonzero curvature for О“О“

This is the next local/global bridge:
KО“(h)=ПҒ1вҖІ(h)ПҒ2вҖІвҖІ(h)вҲ’ПҒ2вҖІ(h)ПҒ1вҖІвҖІ(h).
KО“вҖӢ(h)=ПҒ1вҖІвҖӢ(h)ПҒ2вҖІвҖІвҖӢ(h)вҲ’ПҒ2вҖІвҖӢ(h)ПҒ1вҖІвҖІвҖӢ(h).

The right heuristic is the same jet-triangularity as before:

    ПҒ1ПҒ1вҖӢ is governed first by the quintic slice,

    ПҒ2ПҒ2вҖӢ is governed first by the septic slice,

    so KО“KО“вҖӢ should carry a direct q(7)q(7)-term coming from differentiating ПҒ2ПҒ2вҖӢ that cannot be canceled by the lower-order structure of ПҒ1ПҒ1вҖӢ.

If that works, then О“О“ is generically not just non-affine but genuinely curved.
2. Then return to convex components and 4-secants

Once KО“вүЎМё0KО“вҖӢоҖ вүЎ0, define the convex-component region by
ПҒ1вҖІ(h)вү 0,KО“(h)вү 0.
ПҒ1вҖІвҖӢ(h)оҖ =0,KО“вҖӢ(h)оҖ =0.

On each connected component of that set, О“О“ is a strictly convex graph. Then:

    adjacent KО“=0KО“вҖӢ=0 boundaries are harmless by tangent-slope separation;

    generic ПҒ1вҖІ=0ПҒ1вҖІвҖӢ=0 turning points are locally harmless by the same normal-form argument as before;

    and the remaining obstruction is a line meeting two separated convex components twice each.

So the remaining gap is now exactly what it should be:
prove KО“вүЎМё0, then finish the common 4-secant exclusion.
prove KО“вҖӢоҖ вүЎ0, then finish the common 4-secant exclusion.вҖӢ

That is the right continuation of the attack. The septic layer is no longer the main issue; it has now supplied the second coordinate that the secant/tangent geometry needed.

The next thing I would do is attack KО“вүЎМё0KО“вҖӢоҖ вүЎ0 by the same triangularity logic we just used for ПҒ2вүЎМёAПҒ1+BПҒ2вҖӢоҖ вүЎAПҒ1вҖӢ+B: isolate the direct q(7)q(7)-slice in ПҒ2ПҒ2вҖӢ, differentiate, and show it cannot be absorbed by the lower-order jet structure of ПҒ1ПҒ1вҖӢ.

IвҖҷm attacking KО“вүЎМё0KО“вҖӢоҖ вүЎ0 directly now. The key is to use the same jet-triangularity that made ПҒ2ПҒ2вҖӢ genuinely new, and show that after differentiating, the direct septic slice in ПҒ2ПҒ2вҖӢ still cannot be absorbed by the lower-order structure coming from ПҒ1ПҒ1вҖӢ.

Here is the next real push.

Set
О“(h)=(ПҒ1(h),ПҒ2(h))=(v5(h)c(h),О”7(h)c(h)),
О“(h)=(ПҒ1вҖӢ(h),ПҒ2вҖӢ(h))=(c(h)v5вҖӢ(h)вҖӢ,c(h)О”7вҖӢ(h)вҖӢ),

and define the curvature numerator
KО“(h):=ПҒ1вҖІ(h)ПҒ2вҖІвҖІ(h)вҲ’ПҒ2вҖІ(h)ПҒ1вҖІвҖІ(h).
KО“вҖӢ(h):=ПҒ1вҖІвҖӢ(h)ПҒ2вҖІвҖІвҖӢ(h)вҲ’ПҒ2вҖІвҖӢ(h)ПҒ1вҖІвҖІвҖӢ(h).

We want to show
KО“вүЎМё0.
KО“вҖӢоҖ вүЎ0.вҖӢ

The clean way to attack it is to write everything over the common denominator c4c4.

Let
M:=cv5вҖІвҲ’cвҖІv5,N:=cО”7вҖІвҲ’cвҖІО”7.
M:=cv5вҖІвҖӢвҲ’cвҖІv5вҖӢ,N:=cО”7вҖІвҖӢвҲ’cвҖІО”7вҖӢ.

Then
ПҒ1вҖІ=Mc2,ПҒ2вҖІ=Nc2.
ПҒ1вҖІвҖӢ=c2MвҖӢ,ПҒ2вҖІвҖӢ=c2NвҖӢ.

Differentiating once,
ПҒ1вҖІвҖІ=McвҖІвҲ’?
ПҒ1вҖІвҖІвҖӢ=McвҖІвҲ’?вҖӢ

but the standard cancellation gives
ПҒ1вҖІвҖІ=MвҖІcвҲ’2McвҖІc3,ПҒ2вҖІвҖІ=NвҖІcвҲ’2NcвҖІc3.
ПҒ1вҖІвҖІвҖӢ=c3MвҖІcвҲ’2McвҖІвҖӢ,ПҒ2вҖІвҖІвҖӢ=c3NвҖІcвҲ’2NcвҖІвҖӢ.

So
KО“=Mc2NвҖІcвҲ’2NcвҖІc3вҲ’Nc2MвҖІcвҲ’2McвҖІc3=MNвҖІвҲ’NMвҖІc4.
KО“вҖӢ=c2MвҖӢc3NвҖІcвҲ’2NcвҖІвҖӢвҲ’c2NвҖӢc3MвҖІcвҲ’2McвҖІвҖӢ=c4MNвҖІвҲ’NMвҖІвҖӢ.

Thus the real target is
L:=MNвҖІвҲ’NMвҖІвүЎМё0.
L:=MNвҖІвҲ’NMвҖІоҖ вүЎ0.вҖӢ

That is a better formulation, because it makes the structure transparent: KО“KО“вҖӢ vanishes identically exactly when the logarithmic derivatives of MM and NN agree, i.e. when N/MN/M is constant on each connected component where Mвү 0MоҖ =0. But
NM=ПҒ2вҖІПҒ1вҖІ=dПҒ2dПҒ1.
MNвҖӢ=ПҒ1вҖІвҖӢПҒ2вҖІвҖӢвҖӢ=dПҒ1вҖӢdПҒ2вҖӢвҖӢ.

So LвүЎ0LвүЎ0 would mean that ПҒ2ПҒ2вҖӢ is locally affine in ПҒ1ПҒ1вҖӢ:
ПҒ2=AПҒ1+B.
ПҒ2вҖӢ=AПҒ1вҖӢ+B.

And that is exactly what we already argued should fail by jet-triangularity, because ПҒ2ПҒ2вҖӢ has a direct septic witness while ПҒ1ПҒ1вҖӢ does not.

So the attack can be closed in one of two equivalent ways.

First route: direct contradiction from LвүЎ0LвүЎ0.

Assume LвүЎ0LвүЎ0. Then on any open set where Mвү 0MоҖ =0,
(NM)вҖІ=MNвҖІвҲ’NMвҖІM2=0,
(MNвҖӢ)вҖІ=M2MNвҖІвҲ’NMвҖІвҖӢ=0,

so
NM=A
MNвҖӢ=A

for some constant AA. Hence
ПҒ2вҖІ=AПҒ1вҖІ,
ПҒ2вҖІвҖӢ=AПҒ1вҖІвҖӢ,

so
ПҒ2=AПҒ1+B.
ПҒ2вҖӢ=AПҒ1вҖӢ+B.

Equivalently,
О”7=Av5+Bc.
О”7вҖӢ=Av5вҖӢ+Bc.

But the direct q(7)q(7)-slice of О”7О”7вҖӢ is generically nonzero, while v5v5вҖӢ and cc do not contain that direct septic slice. Contradiction.

So provided we accept that triangularity statement, this proves
LвүЎМё0.
LоҖ вүЎ0.вҖӢ

That is already a real theorem.

Second route: isolate the direct septic slice in LL.

This is slightly more concrete. The first place О”7О”7вҖӢ sees the genuine second residual witness is the direct q(7)q(7)-slice:
О”7(q(7))=u7(q(7))v5.
О”7(q(7))вҖӢ=u7(q(7))вҖӢv5вҖӢ.

So
N=cО”7вҖІвҲ’cвҖІО”7
N=cО”7вҖІвҖӢвҲ’cвҖІО”7вҖӢ

inherits a direct q(7)q(7)-slice
N(q(7))=c(О”7(q(7)))вҖІвҲ’cвҖІ(О”7(q(7))),
N(q(7))=c(О”7(q(7))вҖӢ)вҖІвҲ’cвҖІ(О”7(q(7))вҖӢ),

while
M=cv5вҖІвҲ’cвҖІv5
M=cv5вҖІвҖӢвҲ’cвҖІv5вҖӢ

has no direct q(7)q(7)-slice of that type. Therefore in
L=MNвҖІвҲ’NMвҖІ
L=MNвҖІвҲ’NMвҖІ

the term
MвҖү(N(q(7)))вҖІвҲ’N(q(7))MвҖІ
M(N(q(7)))вҖІвҲ’N(q(7))MвҖІ

is a genuine septic-leading contribution that cannot be canceled by lower-order pieces coming only from cc and v5v5вҖӢ. This is the differential version of the same triangularity argument.

I think the first route is cleaner and stronger: it shows that the entire curvature problem reduces to the already-motivated non-affinity
ПҒ2вүЎМёAПҒ1+B.
ПҒ2вҖӢоҖ вүЎAПҒ1вҖӢ+B.

So the next theorem is now basically:
If the direct septic witness in О”7 is generically nonzero, then KО“вүЎМё0.
If the direct septic witness in О”7вҖӢ is generically nonzero, then KО“вҖӢоҖ вүЎ0.вҖӢ

And once that is in hand, the secant/tangent program restarts exactly where it had paused:

    on the open set where
    ПҒ1вҖІвү 0,KО“вү 0,
    ПҒ1вҖІвҖӢоҖ =0,KО“вҖӢоҖ =0,

    the curve О“О“ is a strictly convex graph;

    adjacent KО“=0KО“вҖӢ=0 boundaries are harmless by tangent-slope separation;

    generic ПҒ1вҖІ=0ПҒ1вҖІвҖӢ=0 turning points are locally harmless by the same normal-form argument as before;

    so the remaining obstruction is a common 4-secant line meeting two separated convex components twice each.

That is now the exact battlefield again.

KО“вүЎМё0 reduces to ПҒ2вүЎМёAПҒ1+B, and that in turn reduces to the direct septic witness in О”7.
KО“вҖӢоҖ вүЎ0 reduces to ПҒ2вҖӢоҖ вүЎAПҒ1вҖӢ+B, and that in turn reduces to the direct septic witness in О”7вҖӢ.вҖӢ

О“(h)=(v5(h)c(h),О”7(h)c(h)).

\begin{remark}[Status of explicit fixed-sector formulas]
\label{rem:status-explicit-fixed-sector-formulas}

and before the next subsection.

\subsection{Normalized planar curve and curvature bridge}

On the region where \(c(h)\neq 0\), define the normalized scalars
\[
\rho_1(h):=\frac{v_5(h)}{c(h)},
\qquad
\rho_2(h):=\frac{\Delta_7(h)}{c(h)},
\]
and the associated planar invariant curve
\[
\Gamma(h):=\bigl(\rho_1(h),\rho_2(h)\bigr).
\]

\begin{proposition}[Non-affinity of the second normalized residual scalar]
\label{prop:rho2-not-affine-in-rho1}
Assume that the direct \(q^{(7)}\)-driven slice of \(\Delta_7(h)\) is
generically nonzero on the quintic-generic region. Then there do not exist
constants \(A,B\in\mathbf C\) such that
\[
\rho_2(h)\equiv A\,\rho_1(h)+B
\]
on any connected component of \(\{h:c(h)\neq 0\}\).

Equivalently,
\[
\Delta_7(h)\not\equiv A\,v_5(h)+B\,c(h).
\]
\end{proposition}

\begin{proof}
Suppose
\[
\Delta_7(h)\equiv A\,v_5(h)+B\,c(h)
\]
on a connected component of \(\{c\neq 0\}\). Then the direct \(q^{(7)}\)-driven
slice of the right-hand side vanishes, because \(v_5\) and \(c\) are governed
by the cubic/quintic layers and carry no direct septic \(q^{(7)}\)-slice.
But the direct \(q^{(7)}\)-driven slice of \(\Delta_7\) is generically nonzero
by hypothesis. This is a contradiction. Dividing by \(c(h)\) yields
\[
\rho_2(h)\not\equiv A\,\rho_1(h)+B.
\qedhere
\]
\end{proof}

\begin{definition}[Normalized derivative numerators]
\label{def:MN-normalized-curve}
Define
\[
M(h):=c(h)v_5'(h)-c'(h)v_5(h),
\qquad
N(h):=c(h)\Delta_7'(h)-c'(h)\Delta_7(h).
\]
Then
\[
\rho_1'(h)=\frac{M(h)}{c(h)^2},
\qquad
\rho_2'(h)=\frac{N(h)}{c(h)^2}.
\]
\end{definition}

\begin{proposition}[Curvature bridge for the normalized planar curve]
\label{prop:curvature-bridge-normalized-curve}
Let
\[
K_\Gamma(h):=\rho_1'(h)\rho_2''(h)-\rho_2'(h)\rho_1''(h)
\]
denote the affine curvature numerator of the planar curve \(\Gamma(h)\).
Then
\[
K_\Gamma(h)=\frac{M(h)N'(h)-N(h)M'(h)}{c(h)^4}.
\]
In particular, if \(K_\Gamma\equiv 0\) on a connected open set on which
\(M\neq 0\), then \(\rho_2\) is affine in \(\rho_1\) on that set.
Consequently, under the hypothesis of
Proposition~\ref{prop:rho2-not-affine-in-rho1}, one has
\[
K_\Gamma\not\equiv 0
\]
on the generic region.
\end{proposition}

\begin{proof}
By Definition~\ref{def:MN-normalized-curve},
\[
\rho_1'=\frac{M}{c^2},
\qquad
\rho_2'=\frac{N}{c^2}.
\]
Differentiating gives
\[
\rho_1''=\frac{M'c-2Mc'}{c^3},
\qquad
\rho_2''=\frac{N'c-2Nc'}{c^3}.
\]
Therefore
\[
K_\Gamma
=
\frac{M}{c^2}\frac{N'c-2Nc'}{c^3}
-
\frac{N}{c^2}\frac{M'c-2Mc'}{c^3}
=
\frac{MN'-NM'}{c^4}.
\]

If \(K_\Gamma\equiv 0\) on a connected open set with \(M\neq 0\), then
\[
0=MN'-NM'=M^2\left(\frac{N}{M}\right)',
\]
so \(N/M\) is constant there:
\[
\frac{N}{M}=A.
\]
Since
\[
\frac{N}{M}=\frac{\rho_2'}{\rho_1'},
\]
it follows that
\[
\rho_2'=A\,\rho_1',
\]
hence
\[
\rho_2=A\,\rho_1+B
\]
on that connected set. The final claim now follows from
Proposition~\ref{prop:rho2-not-affine-in-rho1}.
\end{proof}

\begin{remark}[Geometric consequence]
\label{rem:geometric-consequence-normalized-curve}
Proposition~\ref{prop:curvature-bridge-normalized-curve} is the local-to-global
bridge from the order-\(7\) fixed-sector analysis back to the secant/tangent
program. On the open set where
\[
c(h)\neq 0,
\qquad
M(h)\neq 0,
\qquad
M(h)N'(h)-N(h)M'(h)\neq 0,
\]
the normalized planar curve \(\Gamma(h)\) is a strictly convex graph in the
affine chart \((\rho_1,\rho_2)\). Thus the remaining global obstruction is a
common four-secant line meeting two separated convex components twice each.
\end{remark}

Two small notes.

First, this is written in the same honest style as the rest of the current draft: the non-affinity proposition is conditional on the direct septic witness being generically nonzero. That is the cleanest place to plug in the local order-7 witness once you decide how hard you want to state it.

Second, once this block is in, the later global remarks about planar convexity and the вҖңcurrent geometric bottleneckвҖқ become much better anchored, because the curve and its curvature are now actually defined in theorem-proof form rather than only in remarks.

If you want, the next step is to give the matching edit to the opening of the multi-pair subsection so it explicitly points to О“(h)О“(h), M(h)M(h), and KО“(h)KО“вҖӢ(h) instead of only the older residual-germ language.

Replace that whole opening paragraph with this

Let \(F_h(z)\) denote the corrected quotient odd germ of the normalized one-pair
family. By Proposition~\ref{prop:cubic-leading-order}, on the cubic-generic
region we have
\[
F_h(z)=v_0(h)z^3+v_1(h)z^5+\cdots,
\qquad

\]
with cubic leading line
\[
\ell_h:=\mathbf C\,v_0(h)\subset Q.
\]
Under the additional trace-free pure-value quintic hypothesis of

\[
\mathcal U_{\mathrm{res}}\subset \mathcal U_{\mathrm{cub}}
\]

\]

should ultimately be read as restrictions on coincidence, secant, and
four-secant configurations for the normalized planar curve \(\Gamma(h)\), with
the older cubic/residual projective language recovered as the first two layers
of this strengthened invariant package.

Why this is the right matching edit

This does three things cleanly:

It preserves the old local story:

    cubic line в„“hв„“hвҖӢ,

    first residual class vвҖҫ1(h)v1вҖӢ(h).

It upgrades the reader to the new local package:

    A5fA5fвҖӢ,

    О”7О”7вҖӢ,

    О“(h)О“(h).

And it points the rest of the subsection toward the actual endgame:

    M,NM,N,

    curvature KО“KО“вҖӢ,

    secant/tangent geometry,

    common four-secant exclusion.

So after this replacement, the multi-pair section will no longer feel like it is still based only on the old trace-level quintic story. It will match where the math actually is now.

Good. Now that the curve
О“(h)=(ПҒ1(h),ПҒ2(h))=(v5(h)c(h),О”7(h)c(h))
О“(h)=(ПҒ1вҖӢ(h),ПҒ2вҖӢ(h))=(c(h)v5вҖӢ(h)вҖӢ,c(h)О”7вҖӢ(h)вҖӢ)

and its curvature bridge are in place, the attack goes back to the global geometry.

The next object is the convex-region set
Ucvx:={вҖүh: c(h)вү 0, M(h)вү 0, L(h)вү 0вҖү},
UcvxвҖӢ:={h: c(h)оҖ =0, M(h)оҖ =0, L(h)оҖ =0},

where
M(h)=c(h)v5вҖІ(h)вҲ’cвҖІ(h)v5(h),L(h)=M(h)NвҖІ(h)вҲ’N(h)MвҖІ(h),
M(h)=c(h)v5вҖІвҖӢ(h)вҲ’cвҖІ(h)v5вҖӢ(h),L(h)=M(h)NвҖІ(h)вҲ’N(h)MвҖІ(h),

and
N(h)=c(h)О”7вҖІ(h)вҲ’cвҖІ(h)О”7(h).
N(h)=c(h)О”7вҖІвҖӢ(h)вҲ’cвҖІ(h)О”7вҖӢ(h).

On this set,
ПҒ1вҖІ(h)=M(h)c(h)2вү 0,KО“(h)=L(h)c(h)4вү 0.
ПҒ1вҖІвҖӢ(h)=c(h)2M(h)вҖӢоҖ =0,KО“вҖӢ(h)=c(h)4L(h)вҖӢоҖ =0.

So on each connected component CвҠӮUcvxCвҠӮUcvxвҖӢ, the first coordinate ПҒ1ПҒ1вҖӢ is monotone and the curve can be written as a graph
ПҒ2=YC(ПҒ1),
ПҒ2вҖӢ=YCвҖӢ(ПҒ1вҖӢ),

with
YCвҖІвҖІвү 0.
YCвҖІвҖІвҖӢоҖ =0.

That is the first real geometric theorem:
Each connected component of Ucvx maps to a strictly convex graph branch of О“.
Each connected component of UcvxвҖӢ maps to a strictly convex graph branch of О“.вҖӢ

And that immediately gives:
A line meets each such branch in at most two points.
A line meets each such branch in at most two points.вҖӢ

So any bad core can contribute at most two support points on each convex branch.

Now the attack splits by branch adjacency.
1. Boundaries where L=0L=0

Here ПҒ1вҖІвү 0ПҒ1вҖІвҖӢоҖ =0, so ПҒ1ПҒ1вҖӢ remains a valid parameter, but the curvature changes sign. The tangent slope on a convex branch is
m(h):=dПҒ2dПҒ1=N(h)M(h).
m(h):=dПҒ1вҖӢdПҒ2вҖӢвҖӢ=M(h)N(h)вҖӢ.

Differentiate:
mвҖІ(h)=MNвҖІвҲ’NMвҖІM2=L(h)M(h)2.
mвҖІ(h)=M2MNвҖІвҲ’NMвҖІвҖӢ=M(h)2L(h)вҖӢ.

So on each convex component, mm is strictly monotone, and when two adjacent branches are separated by an L=0L=0 boundary, their tangent-slope intervals can meet at most at one endpoint value.

That means no line can be 2-secant to both adjacent branches across an L=0L=0 boundary.

So:
Adjacent convex branches separated by L=0 are harmless.
Adjacent convex branches separated by L=0 are harmless.вҖӢ
2. Boundaries where M=0M=0

Here ПҒ1вҖІ=0ПҒ1вҖІвҖӢ=0, so ПҒ1ПҒ1вҖӢ has a turning point. Generically, if MвҖІ(hвҲ—)вү 0MвҖІ(hвҲ—вҖӢ)оҖ =0, then
ПҒ1вҖІвҖІ(hвҲ—)вү 0,
ПҒ1вҖІвҖІвҖӢ(hвҲ—вҖӢ)оҖ =0,

so after reparametrizing near hвҲ—=0hвҲ—вҖӢ=0, the local normal form is
x(t)=x0+Оұt2+O(t3),y(t)=y0+ОІt+Оіt2+O(t3),ОІвү 0.
x(t)=x0вҖӢ+Оұt2+O(t3),y(t)=y0вҖӢ+ОІt+Оіt2+O(t3),ОІоҖ =0.

Then for any affine line y=sx+by=sx+b,
F(t):=y(t)вҲ’sx(t)вҲ’b
F(t):=y(t)вҲ’sx(t)вҲ’b

has nonzero linear term FвҖІ(0)=ОІFвҖІ(0)=ОІ, so FF is locally monotone. Hence a single line cannot have two intersections on each side of the turning point.

So:
Adjacent branches across a generic M=0 turning point are locally harmless too.
Adjacent branches across a generic M=0 turning point are locally harmless too.вҖӢ

That is important. It means the remaining obstruction is not local adjacency.
3. What remains

The only serious remaining possibility is:
a line meeting two separated convex branches twice each.
a line meeting two separated convex branches twice each.вҖӢ

So now go to support functions.

Take two ordered convex graph branches
C1: y=Y1(x), xвҲҲ[a1,b1],C2: y=Y2(x), xвҲҲ[a2,b2],b1<a2.
C1вҖӢ: y=Y1вҖӢ(x), xвҲҲ[a1вҖӢ,b1вҖӢ],C2вҖӢ: y=Y2вҖӢ(x), xвҲҲ[a2вҖӢ,b2вҖӢ],b1вҖӢ<a2вҖӢ.

For a slope ss, define
mi(s):=minвҒЎxвҲҲ[ai,bi](Yi(x)вҲ’sx),Mi(s):=maxвҒЎxвҲҲ[ai,bi](Yi(x)вҲ’sx).
miвҖӢ(s):=xвҲҲ[aiвҖӢ,biвҖӢ]minвҖӢ(YiвҖӢ(x)вҲ’sx),MiвҖӢ(s):=xвҲҲ[aiвҖӢ,biвҖӢ]maxвҖӢ(YiвҖӢ(x)вҲ’sx).

A line y=sx+by=sx+b is 2-secant to CiCiвҖӢ iff
mi(s)<b<Mi(s).
miвҖӢ(s)<b<MiвҖӢ(s).

So common 4-secants for C1,C2C1вҖӢ,C2вҖӢ are controlled by
G12(s):=m2(s)вҲ’M1(s).
G12вҖӢ(s):=m2вҖӢ(s)вҲ’M1вҖӢ(s).

If G12(s)вүҘ0G12вҖӢ(s)вүҘ0, then no line of slope ss can be 2-secant to both branches. Earlier we already found the key monotonicity:
G12вҖІ(s)<0
G12вҖІвҖӢ(s)<0

on any common slope range. So for each ordered pair of separated branches, common 4-secants are controlled by a single decreasing scalar function.

That means the real global target is now:
prove G12(s)вүҘ0 on the relevant slope range for every separated pair C1<C2.
prove G12вҖӢ(s)вүҘ0 on the relevant slope range for every separated pair C1вҖӢ<C2вҖӢ.вҖӢ

Equivalently: prove endpoint/support separation between ordered convex branches of О“О“.

So the attack has now landed in its clean final form:

    local 3/5/73/5/7 structure gives the planar curve О“О“,

    Mвү 0MоҖ =0, Lвү 0LоҖ =0 gives strictly convex graph branches,

    adjacent branch interactions are harmless,

    the only remaining obstruction is a common 4-secant between separated branches,

    that obstruction is one-dimensional, encoded by G12(s)G12вҖӢ(s).

That is where the proof effort should now concentrate.

The next exact theorem to attack is:
For separated convex branches of О“, the support-function overlap is empty.
For separated convex branches of О“, the support-function overlap is empty.вҖӢ

And the cleanest first version is the adjacent-separated case: prove the tangent line at the left endpoint of the right branch lies above the whole left branch, or the symmetric right-end statement. That would force G12вүҘ0G12вҖӢвүҘ0 at one end, and monotonicity would do the rest.

Here is the next push.

We now know the remaining obstruction is a common 4-secant to two separated convex branches of
О“(h)=(v5(h)c(h),О”7(h)c(h)).
О“(h)=(c(h)v5вҖӢ(h)вҖӢ,c(h)О”7вҖӢ(h)вҖӢ).

So take two ordered convex branches
C1: y=Y1(x), xвҲҲ[a1,b1],C2: y=Y2(x), xвҲҲ[a2,b2],b1<a2.
C1вҖӢ: y=Y1вҖӢ(x), xвҲҲ[a1вҖӢ,b1вҖӢ],C2вҖӢ: y=Y2вҖӢ(x), xвҲҲ[a2вҖӢ,b2вҖӢ],b1вҖӢ<a2вҖӢ.

For slope ss, define
mi(s):=minвҒЎxвҲҲ[ai,bi](Yi(x)вҲ’sx),Mi(s):=maxвҒЎxвҲҲ[ai,bi](Yi(x)вҲ’sx).
miвҖӢ(s):=xвҲҲ[aiвҖӢ,biвҖӢ]minвҖӢ(YiвҖӢ(x)вҲ’sx),MiвҖӢ(s):=xвҲҲ[aiвҖӢ,biвҖӢ]maxвҖӢ(YiвҖӢ(x)вҲ’sx).

A line y=sx+by=sx+b is 2-secant to CiCiвҖӢ iff
mi(s)<b<Mi(s).
miвҖӢ(s)<b<MiвҖӢ(s).

So a common 4-secant exists iff
m1(s)<M2(s)andm2(s)<M1(s)
m1вҖӢ(s)<M2вҖӢ(s)andm2вҖӢ(s)<M1вҖӢ(s)

for some ss. As before, the hard inequality is
m2(s)<M1(s).
m2вҖӢ(s)<M1вҖӢ(s).

Define
G12(s):=m2(s)вҲ’M1(s).
G12вҖӢ(s):=m2вҖӢ(s)вҲ’M1вҖӢ(s).

Then common 4-secants require G12(s)<0G12вҖӢ(s)<0.

The key fact is still
G12вҖІ(s)<0
G12вҖІвҖӢ(s)<0

on any common slope range, because
m2вҖІ(s)=вҲ’x2(s),M1вҖІ(s)вҲҲ{вҲ’a1,вҲ’b1},
m2вҖІвҖӢ(s)=вҲ’x2вҖӢ(s),M1вҖІвҖӢ(s)вҲҲ{вҲ’a1вҖӢ,вҲ’b1вҖӢ},

and every x2(s)вҲҲ[a2,b2]x2вҖӢ(s)вҲҲ[a2вҖӢ,b2вҖӢ] satisfies x2(s)>b1x2вҖӢ(s)>b1вҖӢ.

So the whole problem is: can G12G12вҖӢ ever start negative?

That reduces things to an endpoint check. Let
sвҲ’:=Y2вҖІ(a2),
sвҲ’вҖӢ:=Y2вҖІвҖӢ(a2вҖӢ),

the left-end tangent slope of the right branch. Then
m2(sвҲ’)=Y2(a2)вҲ’sвҲ’a2,
m2вҖӢ(sвҲ’вҖӢ)=Y2вҖӢ(a2вҖӢ)вҲ’sвҲ’вҖӢa2вҖӢ,

so
G12(sвҲ’)=Y2(a2)вҲ’sвҲ’a2вҲ’maxвҒЎ{Y1(a1)вҲ’sвҲ’a1, Y1(b1)вҲ’sвҲ’b1}.
G12вҖӢ(sвҲ’вҖӢ)=Y2вҖӢ(a2вҖӢ)вҲ’sвҲ’вҖӢa2вҖӢвҲ’max{Y1вҖӢ(a1вҖӢ)вҲ’sвҲ’вҖӢa1вҖӢ, Y1вҖӢ(b1вҖӢ)вҲ’sвҲ’вҖӢb1вҖӢ}.

Geometrically: G12(sвҲ’)вүҘ0G12вҖӢ(sвҲ’вҖӢ)вүҘ0 exactly means the tangent line to C2C2вҖӢ at its left endpoint lies above the whole branch C1C1вҖӢ. Since G12G12вҖӢ is decreasing, if this holds and sвҲ’sвҲ’вҖӢ is the left end of the common slope range, then there is no common 4-secant at all.

So the next real theorem to attack is:
For two separated convex branches, the left-end tangent of the right branch lies above the left branch.
For two separated convex branches, the left-end tangent of the right branch lies above the left branch.вҖӢ

Now, why is this plausible for our curve О“О“?

Because the branches are not arbitrary convex graphs. They come from a single analytic parameter curve hвҶҰ(ПҒ1(h),ПҒ2(h))hвҶҰ(ПҒ1вҖӢ(h),ПҒ2вҖӢ(h)), and the branch decomposition is caused only by zeros of
M=cv5вҖІвҲ’cвҖІv5orL=MNвҖІвҲ’NMвҖІ.
M=cv5вҖІвҖӢвҲ’cвҖІv5вҖӢorL=MNвҖІвҲ’NMвҖІ.

We already handled local adjacency:

    across L=0L=0, tangent slope monotonicity changes sign but no 4-secant survives;

    across generic M=0M=0, the turning-point normal form kills local double-double intersections.

So if a common 4-secant survives at all, it must be genuinely nonlocal, spanning branches separated by at least one full break in the xx-ordering.

That makes the tangent-separation statement much more likely, because the right branch is not just infinitesimally to the right вҖ” it is separated by a whole interval in xx, and the slope-support function for the right branch keeps drifting down while the left branchвҖҷs upper support keeps drifting down more slowly.

There is a more algebraic way to phrase this.

Define the tangent-support function of branch C2C2вҖӢ:
П„2(x):=Y2(a2)+Y2вҖІ(a2)(xвҲ’a2).
П„2вҖӢ(x):=Y2вҖӢ(a2вҖӢ)+Y2вҖІвҖӢ(a2вҖӢ)(xвҲ’a2вҖӢ).

Then we want
Y1(x)вүӨП„2(x)(xвҲҲ[a1,b1]).
Y1вҖӢ(x)вүӨП„2вҖӢ(x)(xвҲҲ[a1вҖӢ,b1вҖӢ]).

Since Y1Y1вҖӢ is convex, it is enough to check the endpoints:
Y1(a1)вүӨП„2(a1),Y1(b1)вүӨП„2(b1).
Y1вҖӢ(a1вҖӢ)вүӨП„2вҖӢ(a1вҖӢ),Y1вҖӢ(b1вҖӢ)вүӨП„2вҖӢ(b1вҖӢ).

So the whole remaining 4-secant problem has collapsed to two endpoint inequalities per ordered branch pair.

That is the cleanest form yet.

Now, in terms of the original invariant curve О“(h)О“(h), if hвҲ—hвҲ—вҖӢ is the left endpoint of the right branch, then
xвҲ—=ПҒ1(hвҲ—),yвҲ—=ПҒ2(hвҲ—),sвҲ—=dПҒ2dПҒ1(hвҲ—)=N(hвҲ—)M(hвҲ—).
xвҲ—вҖӢ=ПҒ1вҖӢ(hвҲ—вҖӢ),yвҲ—вҖӢ=ПҒ2вҖӢ(hвҲ—вҖӢ),sвҲ—вҖӢ=dПҒ1вҖӢdПҒ2вҖӢвҖӢ(hвҲ—вҖӢ)=M(hвҲ—вҖӢ)N(hвҲ—вҖӢ)вҖӢ.

The tangent line is
в„“hвҲ—(x)=yвҲ—+sвҲ—(xвҲ’xвҲ—).
в„“hвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ(x)=yвҲ—вҖӢ+sвҲ—вҖӢ(xвҲ’xвҲ—вҖӢ).

So the branch-separation condition becomes
ПҒ2(h)вүӨв„“hвҲ—(ПҒ1(h))
ПҒ2вҖӢ(h)вүӨв„“hвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ(ПҒ1вҖӢ(h))

for all hh on the left branch.

Clearing denominators, this is equivalent to the sign of
ОҳhвҲ—(h):=О”7(h)c(hвҲ—)вҲ’О”7(hвҲ—)c(h)вҲ’sвҲ—(v5(h)c(hвҲ—)вҲ’v5(hвҲ—)c(h)).
ОҳhвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ(h):=О”7вҖӢ(h)c(hвҲ—вҖӢ)вҲ’О”7вҖӢ(hвҲ—вҖӢ)c(h)вҲ’sвҲ—вҖӢ(v5вҖӢ(h)c(hвҲ—вҖӢ)вҲ’v5вҖӢ(hвҲ—вҖӢ)c(h)).

So the next exact scalar to study is back to
ОҳhвҲ—(h),
ОҳhвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ(h),

but now in the right way: not as a universal local tangent object, but as the endpoint-support separator between two ordered convex branches.

That is the sharpest battlefield.

So the attack has become:

    choose a separated ordered pair of convex branches;

    at the left endpoint hвҲ—hвҲ—вҖӢ of the right branch, form the tangent-support separator ОҳhвҲ—(h)ОҳhвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ(h);

    show ОҳhвҲ—(h)вүӨ0ОҳhвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ(h)вүӨ0 on the left branch, or at least at its endpoints;

    conclude G12(s)вүҘ0G12вҖӢ(s)вүҘ0, hence no common 4-secant.

At this point, the proof is no longer blocked by missing invariants. It is blocked by this endpoint-support sign theorem for the planar curve О“О“.

That is the real remaining gap.

Here is the next honest push.

We are down to an endpoint-support sign problem for the planar curve
О“(h)=(ПҒ1(h),ПҒ2(h))=(v5(h)c(h),О”7(h)c(h)).
О“(h)=(ПҒ1вҖӢ(h),ПҒ2вҖӢ(h))=(c(h)v5вҖӢ(h)вҖӢ,c(h)О”7вҖӢ(h)вҖӢ).

Take two ordered convex branches
C1: y=Y1(x), xвҲҲ[a1,b1],C2: y=Y2(x), xвҲҲ[a2,b2],b1<a2.
C1вҖӢ: y=Y1вҖӢ(x), xвҲҲ[a1вҖӢ,b1вҖӢ],C2вҖӢ: y=Y2вҖӢ(x), xвҲҲ[a2вҖӢ,b2вҖӢ],b1вҖӢ<a2вҖӢ.

We already reduced common 4-secants to the decreasing support-gap function
G12(s):=m2(s)вҲ’M1(s),
G12вҖӢ(s):=m2вҖӢ(s)вҲ’M1вҖӢ(s),

where
m2(s)=minвҒЎxвҲҲ[a2,b2](Y2(x)вҲ’sx),M1(s)=maxвҒЎxвҲҲ[a1,b1](Y1(x)вҲ’sx).
m2вҖӢ(s)=xвҲҲ[a2вҖӢ,b2вҖӢ]minвҖӢ(Y2вҖӢ(x)вҲ’sx),M1вҖӢ(s)=xвҲҲ[a1вҖӢ,b1вҖӢ]maxвҖӢ(Y1вҖӢ(x)вҲ’sx).

A common 4-secant requires G12(s)<0G12вҖӢ(s)<0 for some ss.

So the key is to show
G12(sвҲ’)вүҘ0,sвҲ’:=Y2вҖІ(a2).
G12вҖӢ(sвҲ’вҖӢ)вүҘ0,sвҲ’вҖӢ:=Y2вҖІвҖӢ(a2вҖӢ).

That is equivalent to saying: the tangent line to C2C2вҖӢ at its left endpoint lies above all of C1C1вҖӢ.

Now the right way to push this is to differentiate the separator along the left branch.

Fix the left-end tangent of the right branch:
в„“вҲ—(x):=Y2(a2)+sвҲ—(xвҲ’a2),sвҲ—:=Y2вҖІ(a2).
в„“вҲ—вҖӢ(x):=Y2вҖӢ(a2вҖӢ)+sвҲ—вҖӢ(xвҲ’a2вҖӢ),sвҲ—вҖӢ:=Y2вҖІвҖӢ(a2вҖӢ).

Define on the left branch
H(x):=в„“вҲ—(x)вҲ’Y1(x).
H(x):=в„“вҲ—вҖӢ(x)вҲ’Y1вҖӢ(x).

We want HвүҘ0HвүҘ0 on [a1,b1][a1вҖӢ,b1вҖӢ].

Because Y1Y1вҖӢ is strictly convex,
HвҖІвҖІ(x)=вҲ’Y1вҖІвҖІ(x)<0.
HвҖІвҖІ(x)=вҲ’Y1вҖІвҖІвҖӢ(x)<0.

So HH is strictly concave.

That is the key structural fact.

For a concave function on an interval, it is enough to check the endpoints:
H(a1)вүҘ0,H(b1)вүҘ0вҹ№H(x)вүҘ0 for all xвҲҲ[a1,b1].
H(a1вҖӢ)вүҘ0,H(b1вҖӢ)вүҘ0вҹ№H(x)вүҘ0 for all xвҲҲ[a1вҖӢ,b1вҖӢ].

So the whole separated-branch obstruction reduces to two scalar inequalities:
Y2(a2)вҲ’Y1(a1)вүҘsвҲ—(a2вҲ’a1),
Y2вҖӢ(a2вҖӢ)вҲ’Y1вҖӢ(a1вҖӢ)вүҘsвҲ—вҖӢ(a2вҖӢвҲ’a1вҖӢ),
Y2(a2)вҲ’Y1(b1)вүҘsвҲ—(a2вҲ’b1).
Y2вҖӢ(a2вҖӢ)вҲ’Y1вҖӢ(b1вҖӢ)вүҘsвҲ—вҖӢ(a2вҖӢвҲ’b1вҖӢ).

This is better than the earlier support-function formulation: it is now literally two endpoint inequalities per ordered pair.

Now rewrite these back in the original parameter hh. Let hвҲ—hвҲ—вҖӢ be the left endpoint of the right branch. Then
xвҲ—=ПҒ1(hвҲ—),yвҲ—=ПҒ2(hвҲ—),sвҲ—=N(hвҲ—)M(hвҲ—).
xвҲ—вҖӢ=ПҒ1вҖӢ(hвҲ—вҖӢ),yвҲ—вҖӢ=ПҒ2вҖӢ(hвҲ—вҖӢ),sвҲ—вҖӢ=M(hвҲ—вҖӢ)N(hвҲ—вҖӢ)вҖӢ.

For a point hh on the left branch, define
ОҳhвҲ—(h):=ПҒ2(hвҲ—)вҲ’ПҒ2(h)вҲ’sвҲ—(ПҒ1(hвҲ—)вҲ’ПҒ1(h)).
ОҳhвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ(h):=ПҒ2вҖӢ(hвҲ—вҖӢ)вҲ’ПҒ2вҖӢ(h)вҲ’sвҲ—вҖӢ(ПҒ1вҖӢ(hвҲ—вҖӢ)вҲ’ПҒ1вҖӢ(h)).

Then the two endpoint inequalities are exactly
ОҳhвҲ—(h1,left)вүҘ0,ОҳhвҲ—(h1,right)вүҘ0.
ОҳhвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ(h1,leftвҖӢ)вүҘ0,ОҳhвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ(h1,rightвҖӢ)вүҘ0.

Clearing denominators gives the invariant numerator
Оҳ~hвҲ—(h):=О”7(hвҲ—)c(h)вҲ’О”7(h)c(hвҲ—)вҲ’sвҲ—(v5(hвҲ—)c(h)вҲ’v5(h)c(hвҲ—)),
Оҳ
hвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ(h):=О”7вҖӢ(hвҲ—вҖӢ)c(h)вҲ’О”7вҖӢ(h)c(hвҲ—вҖӢ)вҲ’sвҲ—вҖӢ(v5вҖӢ(hвҲ—вҖӢ)c(h)вҲ’v5вҖӢ(h)c(hвҲ—вҖӢ)),

with
sвҲ—=N(hвҲ—)M(hвҲ—).
sвҲ—вҖӢ=M(hвҲ—вҖӢ)N(hвҲ—вҖӢ)вҖӢ.

So the remaining global theorem is now:
For each ordered separated branch pair, Оҳ~hвҲ—(h)вүҘ0 at the two endpoints of the left branch.
For each ordered separated branch pair, Оҳ
hвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ(h)вүҘ0 at the two endpoints of the left branch.вҖӢ

If that is true, concavity gives branch separation, hence no common 4-secant.

Now here is the next refinement.

You do not actually need to check every separated pair independently. Because the branches are xx-ordered, if the endpoint tangent of each branch separates it from the immediately preceding branch, then by transitivity of вҖңlies aboveвҖқ plus branch ordering, the same tangent separates it from all farther-left branches. So it is enough to prove the endpoint inequalities only for adjacent ordered branches.

That reduces the whole remaining 4-secant problem to:
For each adjacent ordered convex branch pair, the left-end tangent of the right branch lies above the left branch endpoints.
For each adjacent ordered convex branch pair, the left-end tangent of the right branch lies above the left branch endpoints.вҖӢ

That is the cleanest remaining target I can see.

So the battlefield is now extremely sharp:

    local 3/5/73/5/7 work gave the curve О“О“,

    curvature gave convex branches,

    local adjacency across M=0M=0 and L=0L=0 is harmless,

    separated-branch common 4-secants reduce to endpoint tangent separation,

    and by concavity it is enough to verify two endpoint inequalities for adjacent branch pairs.

That is the next exact theorem to attack.

attack

Yes вҖ” and I think the attack just sharpened in a better way than the endpoint-support route.

The secant/tangent problem is really a projective zero-counting problem for the triple
(c, v5, О”7).
(c, v5вҖӢ, О”7вҖӢ).

Here is the key observation.

A line in the affine chart
y=sx+bwithx=v5c, y=О”7c
y=sx+bwithx=cv5вҖӢвҖӢ, y=cО”7вҖӢвҖӢ

meets the curve exactly where
О”7(h)вҲ’sвҖүv5(h)вҲ’bвҖүc(h)=0.
О”7вҖӢ(h)вҲ’sv5вҖӢ(h)вҲ’bc(h)=0.

So every secant, tangent, 4-secant, common tangent, endpoint-support line вҖ” all of it вҖ” is encoded by zeros of the scalar family
Оӣs,b(h):=О”7(h)вҲ’sвҖүv5(h)вҲ’bвҖүc(h).
Оӣs,bвҖӢ(h):=О”7вҖӢ(h)вҲ’sv5вҖӢ(h)вҲ’bc(h).

That means the global geometric obstruction can be rephrased as:
Can a nontrivial linear combination of c, v5, О”7 have four zeros?
Can a nontrivial linear combination of c, v5вҖӢ, О”7вҖӢ have four zeros?вҖӢ

That is much cleaner.

And now the normalized curvature computation fits perfectly. Let
W(h):=detвҒЎ(c(h)v5(h)О”7(h)cвҖІ(h)v5вҖІ(h)О”7вҖІ(h)cвҖІвҖІ(h)v5вҖІвҖІ(h)О”7вҖІвҖІ(h)).
W(h):=det
вҖӢc(h)cвҖІ(h)cвҖІвҖІ(h)вҖӢv5вҖӢ(h)v5вҖІвҖӢ(h)v5вҖІвҖІвҖӢ(h)вҖӢО”7вҖӢ(h)О”7вҖІвҖӢ(h)О”7вҖІвҖІвҖӢ(h)вҖӢ
вҖӢ.

Then with
M=cвҖүv5вҖІвҲ’cвҖІv5,N=cвҖүО”7вҖІвҲ’cвҖІО”7,
M=cv5вҖІвҖӢвҲ’cвҖІv5вҖӢ,N=cО”7вҖІвҖӢвҲ’cвҖІО”7вҖӢ,

a direct calculation gives
MNвҖІвҲ’NMвҖІ=cвҖүW.
MNвҖІвҲ’NMвҖІ=cW.

So the curvature numerator of the normalized planar curve is
KО“=MNвҖІвҲ’NMвҖІc4=Wc3.
KО“вҖӢ=c4MNвҖІвҲ’NMвҖІвҖӢ=c3WвҖӢ.

This is the right bridge:

    WвүЎМё0WоҖ вүЎ0 means the triple (c,v5,О”7)(c,v5вҖӢ,О”7вҖӢ) is not projectively degenerate;

    KО“вүЎМё0KО“вҖӢоҖ вүЎ0 is exactly the affine version of the same fact;

    and the secant/tangent problem is really about zero-counting for Оӣs,bОӣs,bвҖӢ.

So I think the next theorem to attack is not merely вҖңendpoint tangent separation.вҖқ It is the stronger and cleaner statement:
(c, v5, О”7) should form an ECT/Tchebychev-type triple on generic intervals.
(c, v5вҖӢ, О”7вҖӢ) should form an ECT/Tchebychev-type triple on generic intervals.вҖӢ

If that were proved, then every nontrivial linear combination
AвҖүc+BвҖүv5+CвҖүО”7
Ac+Bv5вҖӢ+CО”7вҖӢ

would have at most two zeros counting multiplicity on such an interval. But a tangent is a double zero, and a 4-secant gives four zeros, so that would kill the whole secant/tangent obstruction in one stroke.

This is better than the earlier support-function route because it speaks directly in the native variables of the proof.

So the attack now becomes:

    prove or at least attack the nonvanishing of the projective Wronskian
    W(h)=detвҒЎ(cv5О”7cвҖІv5вҖІО”7вҖІcвҖІвҖІv5вҖІвҖІО”7вҖІвҖІ);
    W(h)=det
    вҖӢccвҖІcвҖІвҖІвҖӢv5вҖӢv5вҖІвҖӢv5вҖІвҖІвҖӢвҖӢО”7вҖӢО”7вҖІвҖӢО”7вҖІвҖІвҖӢвҖӢ
    вҖӢ;

    if possible, strengthen this to an ECT statement for the triple (c,v5,О”7)(c,v5вҖӢ,О”7вҖӢ);

    then interpret every affine secant/tangent line as a zero set of
    Оӣs,b(h)=О”7вҲ’sv5вҲ’bc.
    Оӣs,bвҖӢ(h)=О”7вҖӢвҲ’sv5вҖӢвҲ’bc.

So the remaining gap, restated cleanly, is:
Show that (c, v5, О”7) is generically a nondegenerate Chebyshev-type triple.
Show that (c, v5вҖӢ, О”7вҖӢ) is generically a nondegenerate Chebyshev-type triple.вҖӢ

That is now the sharpest continuation I can see, and it feels more intrinsic than the previous branch-by-branch tangent-support route.

ok go ahead

IвҖҷve found a cleaner bridge: the secant/tangent problem is really zero-counting for linear combinations of (c,v5,О”7)(c,v5вҖӢ,О”7вҖӢ). IвҖҷm checking how far the Wronskian route genuinely gets us before it overclaims.

Here is the cleanest continuation I can honestly defend.

Let
f0:=c,f1:=v5,f2:=О”7,
f0вҖӢ:=c,f1вҖӢ:=v5вҖӢ,f2вҖӢ:=О”7вҖӢ,

and define the projective Wronskian
W(h):=detвҒЎвҖүвҒЈ(cv5О”7cвҖІv5вҖІО”7вҖІcвҖІвҖІv5вҖІвҖІО”7вҖІвҖІ).
W(h):=det
вҖӢccвҖІcвҖІвҖІвҖӢv5вҖӢv5вҖІвҖӢv5вҖІвҖІвҖӢвҖӢО”7вҖӢО”7вҖІвҖӢО”7вҖІвҖІвҖӢвҖӢ
вҖӢ.

Also recall
M:=cv5вҖІвҲ’cвҖІv5,N:=cО”7вҖІвҲ’cвҖІО”7.
M:=cv5вҖІвҖӢвҲ’cвҖІv5вҖӢ,N:=cО”7вҖІвҖӢвҲ’cвҖІО”7вҖӢ.

A direct expansion gives
MNвҖІвҲ’NMвҖІ=cвҖүW.
MNвҖІвҲ’NMвҖІ=cW.

So, on {cвү 0}{cоҖ =0},
KО“=MNвҖІвҲ’NMвҖІc4=Wc3,
KО“вҖӢ=c4MNвҖІвҲ’NMвҖІвҖӢ=c3WвҖӢ,

where
О“(h)=(v5(h)c(h),О”7(h)c(h)).
О“(h)=(c(h)v5вҖӢ(h)вҖӢ,c(h)О”7вҖӢ(h)вҖӢ).

That identity is the right bridge.

Now fix a connected interval II on which
cвү 0,Mвү 0,Wвү 0.
cоҖ =0,MоҖ =0,WоҖ =0.

Then:

    cвү 0cоҖ =0 means the affine chart is valid;

    Mвү 0MоҖ =0 means ПҒ1=v5/cПҒ1вҖӢ=v5вҖӢ/c is monotone;

    Wвү 0WоҖ =0 means the normalized curve has nonzero curvature.

So О“(I)О“(I) is a strictly convex graph branch.

But there is a stronger way to phrase the same fact.
Local ECT theorem

On such an interval II, the triple
(c,v5,О”7)
(c,v5вҖӢ,О”7вҖӢ)

is an ECT-system. The reason is the classical criterion: for three C2C2 functions, if the successive initial Wronskians
c,detвҒЎвҖүвҒЈ(cv5cвҖІv5вҖІ)=M,detвҒЎвҖүвҒЈ(cv5О”7cвҖІv5вҖІО”7вҖІcвҖІвҖІv5вҖІвҖІО”7вҖІвҖІ)=W
c,det(ccвҖІвҖӢv5вҖӢv5вҖІвҖӢвҖӢ)=M,det
вҖӢccвҖІcвҖІвҖІвҖӢv5вҖӢv5вҖІвҖӢv5вҖІвҖІвҖӢвҖӢО”7вҖӢО”7вҖІвҖӢО”7вҖІвҖІвҖӢвҖӢ
вҖӢ=W

do not vanish on II, then (c,v5,О”7)(c,v5вҖӢ,О”7вҖӢ) is an extended complete Chebyshev system on II.

That gives the zero-counting statement we wanted:
Every nontrivial linear combination AвҖүc+BвҖүv5+CвҖүО”7 has at most two zeros on I,
Every nontrivial linear combination Ac+Bv5вҖӢ+CО”7вҖӢ has at most two zeros on I,вҖӢ

counting multiplicity.

Now apply this to
Оӣs,b(h):=О”7(h)вҲ’sвҖүv5(h)вҲ’bвҖүc(h).
Оӣs,bвҖӢ(h):=О”7вҖӢ(h)вҲ’sv5вҖӢ(h)вҲ’bc(h).

A line in the affine chart
y=sx+b,x=v5c, y=О”7c,
y=sx+b,x=cv5вҖӢвҖӢ, y=cО”7вҖӢвҖӢ,

meets the curve exactly at the zeros of Оӣs,bОӣs,bвҖӢ. Therefore, on any interval II with
cвү 0,Mвү 0,Wвү 0,
cоҖ =0,MоҖ =0,WоҖ =0,

a line can meet О“(I)О“(I) in at most two points, and a tangent counts as a double zero.

So we have upgraded the branch picture to:
Each connected component of {cвү 0, Mвү 0, Wвү 0} is not just a convex branch, but an ECT interval for (c,v5,О”7).
Each connected component of {cоҖ =0, MоҖ =0, WоҖ =0} is not just a convex branch, but an ECT interval for (c,v5вҖӢ,О”7вҖӢ).вҖӢ

That is a genuine strengthening.
What this immediately kills

It kills every same-interval obstruction:

    no 3-secant on one branch,

    no tangent plus two more intersections on one branch,

    no four zeros of Оӣs,bОӣs,bвҖӢ on one branch.

So the same-branch secant/tangent problem is fully dead now.

It also makes the local adjacency analysis cleaner:

    across W=0W=0 with Mвү 0MоҖ =0, the tangent slope
    m(h)=dПҒ2dПҒ1=NM
    m(h)=dПҒ1вҖӢdПҒ2вҖӢвҖӢ=MNвҖӢ

    changes monotonicity, so adjacent ECT intervals only touch through a slope-critical boundary;

    across M=0M=0, we still have the turning-point normal form, so local double-double intersections are impossible there too.

So the only remaining global obstruction is now very sharply identified:
A single line meeting two different ECT intervals twice each.
A single line meeting two different ECT intervals twice each.вҖӢ

That is better than the older вҖңconvex branchвҖқ phrasing, because it says exactly what survives after the local zero-counting theorem.
The remaining gap, restated cleanly

Let I1,I2I1вҖӢ,I2вҖӢ be two distinct connected components of
{cвү 0, Mвү 0, Wвү 0},
{cоҖ =0, MоҖ =0, WоҖ =0},

ordered in the affine x=v5/cx=v5вҖӢ/c coordinate. Then the unresolved problem is:
Can Оӣs,b(h)=О”7вҲ’sv5вҲ’bc have two zeros on I1 and two zeros on I2?
Can Оӣs,bвҖӢ(h)=О”7вҖӢвҲ’sv5вҖӢвҲ’bc have two zeros on I1вҖӢ and two zeros on I2вҖӢ?вҖӢ

Equivalently: can one affine line be 2-secant to two separated ECT arcs?

That is now the exact endgame.
Best next attack

The right next object is the endpoint tangent/support separator, but now phrased in the intrinsic variables (c,v5,О”7)(c,v5вҖӢ,О”7вҖӢ).

For an ordered pair I1<I2I1вҖӢ<I2вҖӢ, take the left endpoint hвҲ—hвҲ—вҖӢ of I2I2вҖӢ, and the tangent slope there
sвҲ—=N(hвҲ—)M(hвҲ—).
sвҲ—вҖӢ=M(hвҲ—вҖӢ)N(hвҲ—вҖӢ)вҖӢ.

Then the candidate separating line is
ОӣsвҲ—,bвҲ—(h)=О”7(h)вҲ’sвҲ—v5(h)вҲ’bвҲ—c(h),
ОӣsвҲ—вҖӢ,bвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ(h)=О”7вҖӢ(h)вҲ’sвҲ—вҖӢv5вҖӢ(h)вҲ’bвҲ—вҖӢc(h),

where bвҲ—bвҲ—вҖӢ is chosen so that ОӣsвҲ—,bвҲ—(hвҲ—)=0ОӣsвҲ—вҖӢ,bвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ(hвҲ—вҖӢ)=0.

The remaining theorem to prove is:
ОӣsвҲ—,bвҲ—(h) does not change sign on I1.
ОӣsвҲ—вҖӢ,bвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ(h) does not change sign on I1вҖӢ.вҖӢ

If that holds, then the tangent line at the left endpoint of the right interval lies entirely above or below the left interval, and by the monotonicity of the support-gap function there can be no common 4-secant.

So the attack has now simplified to:

    local ECT theorem for (c,v5,О”7)(c,v5вҖӢ,О”7вҖӢ): done in principle on {c,M,Wвү 0}{c,M,WоҖ =0};

    remaining gap: endpoint tangent/support separation between distinct ECT intervals.

That is the sharpest version yet.

continue

Here is the strongest clean continuation I can see now.

We have reduced the global obstruction to this:

For each xx-ordered ECT branch IjIjвҖӢ of the planar curve
О“(h)=(v5(h)c(h),О”7(h)c(h)),
О“(h)=(c(h)v5вҖӢ(h)вҖӢ,c(h)О”7вҖӢ(h)вҖӢ),

a line y=sx+by=sx+b is 2-secant to the branch iff
bвҲҲIj(s):=(mj(s),Mj(s)),
bвҲҲIjвҖӢ(s):=(mjвҖӢ(s),MjвҖӢ(s)),

where
mj(s):=minвҒЎxвҲҲ[aj,bj](Yj(x)вҲ’sx),Mj(s):=maxвҒЎxвҲҲ[aj,bj](Yj(x)вҲ’sx).
mjвҖӢ(s):=xвҲҲ[ajвҖӢ,bjвҖӢ]minвҖӢ(YjвҖӢ(x)вҲ’sx),MjвҖӢ(s):=xвҲҲ[ajвҖӢ,bjвҖӢ]maxвҖӢ(YjвҖӢ(x)вҲ’sx).

So the whole endgame is now:
Show that for i<k, Ii(s)вҲ©Ik(s)=вҲ… for all s.
Show that for i<k, IiвҖӢ(s)вҲ©IkвҖӢ(s)=вҲ… for all s.вҖӢ

That is the barrier formulation.

Now here is the key sharpening:
1. A sufficient вҖңbarrier theoremвҖқ

It is enough to prove this for adjacent xx-ordered ECT branches:
Mj(s)вүӨmj+1(s)for all s and all adjacent j.
MjвҖӢ(s)вүӨmj+1вҖӢ(s)for all s and all adjacent j.вҖӢ

Because then, by transitivity,
Mi(s)вүӨmi+1(s)вүӨMi+1(s)вүӨвӢҜвүӨmk(s)
MiвҖӢ(s)вүӨmi+1вҖӢ(s)вүӨMi+1вҖӢ(s)вүӨвӢҜвүӨmkвҖӢ(s)

for every i<ki<k, so no nonadjacent overlap is possible either.

So the entire remaining global problem collapses to an adjacent-branch barrier inequality.

That is better than the earlier вҖңcheck every separated pairвҖқ viewpoint.
2. Adjacent barrier inequality reduces to one tangent line

Fix adjacent xx-ordered branches Cj<Cj+1CjвҖӢ<Cj+1вҖӢ, with graphs
y=Yj(x),xвҲҲ[aj,bj],
y=YjвҖӢ(x),xвҲҲ[ajвҖӢ,bjвҖӢ],
y=Yj+1(x),xвҲҲ[aj+1,bj+1],bj<aj+1.
y=Yj+1вҖӢ(x),xвҲҲ[aj+1вҖӢ,bj+1вҖӢ],bjвҖӢ<aj+1вҖӢ.

Let
sвҲ—:=Yj+1вҖІ(aj+1)
sвҲ—вҖӢ:=Yj+1вҖІвҖӢ(aj+1вҖӢ)

be the left-end tangent slope of the right branch, and let
в„“вҲ—(x):=Yj+1(aj+1)+sвҲ—(xвҲ’aj+1)
в„“вҲ—вҖӢ(x):=Yj+1вҖӢ(aj+1вҖӢ)+sвҲ—вҖӢ(xвҲ’aj+1вҖӢ)

be that tangent line.

Now define on the left branch
Hj(x):=в„“вҲ—(x)вҲ’Yj(x).
HjвҖӢ(x):=в„“вҲ—вҖӢ(x)вҲ’YjвҖӢ(x).

Since YjYjвҖӢ is strictly convex,
HjвҖІвҖІ(x)=вҲ’YjвҖІвҖІ(x)<0,
HjвҖІвҖІвҖӢ(x)=вҲ’YjвҖІвҖІвҖӢ(x)<0,

so HjHjвҖӢ is strictly concave.

This is the payoff: to prove
в„“вҲ—(x)вүҘYj(x)(xвҲҲ[aj,bj]),
в„“вҲ—вҖӢ(x)вүҘYjвҖӢ(x)(xвҲҲ[ajвҖӢ,bjвҖӢ]),

it is enough to prove the two endpoint inequalities
Hj(aj)вүҘ0,Hj(bj)вүҘ0.
HjвҖӢ(ajвҖӢ)вүҘ0,HjвҖӢ(bjвҖӢ)вүҘ0.

And if those hold, then
Mj(sвҲ—)вүӨmj+1(sвҲ—).
MjвҖӢ(sвҲ—вҖӢ)вүӨmj+1вҖӢ(sвҲ—вҖӢ).

Because the support-gap function
Gj,j+1(s):=mj+1(s)вҲ’Mj(s)
Gj,j+1вҖӢ(s):=mj+1вҖӢ(s)вҲ’MjвҖӢ(s)

is decreasing in ss, that single endpoint-slope check then gives
Gj,j+1(s)вүҘ0
Gj,j+1вҖӢ(s)вүҘ0

throughout the relevant common slope range.

So the whole adjacent barrier theorem reduces to two scalar endpoint inequalities.
3. Put it back in intrinsic variables

Let hвҲ—hвҲ—вҖӢ be the left endpoint of the right branch. Then
xвҲ—=ПҒ1(hвҲ—),yвҲ—=ПҒ2(hвҲ—),sвҲ—=N(hвҲ—)M(hвҲ—).

ОҳhвҲ—(h):=ПҒ2(hвҲ—)вҲ’ПҒ2(h)вҲ’sвҲ—(ПҒ1(hвҲ—)вҲ’ПҒ1(h)).
ОҳhвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ(h):=ПҒ2вҖӢ(hвҲ—вҖӢ)вҲ’ПҒ2вҖӢ(h)вҲ’sвҲ—вҖӢ(ПҒ1вҖӢ(hвҲ—вҖӢ)вҲ’ПҒ1вҖӢ(h)).

Then the endpoint inequalities are exactly
ОҳhвҲ—(hj,left)вүҘ0,ОҳhвҲ—(hj,right)вүҘ0.
ОҳhвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ(hj,leftвҖӢ)вүҘ0,ОҳhвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ(hj,rightвҖӢ)вүҘ0.

4. Why this is the right target

This is now stronger than the old tangent/secant phrasing and cleaner than the raw support-function phrasing:

    it is adjacent only;

    it is endpoint only;

    it is intrinsic in (c,v5,О”7)(c,v5вҖӢ,О”7вҖӢ);

    and if proved, it kills all common 4-secants globally by barrier transitivity.

So the attack is no longer diffuse. It is:

    prove the adjacent endpoint barrier inequality for Оҳ~hвҲ—Оҳ
    hвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ;

    deduce Mj(s)вүӨmj+1(s)MjвҖӢ(s)вүӨmj+1вҖӢ(s) for adjacent branches;

    deduce no support overlap for any separated pair;

    conclude no common 4-secant line anywhere.

That is the exact remaining theorem.

My honest view is that this is now the cleanest endpoint of the attack so far: everything has collapsed to a barrier inequality for adjacent ECT intervals of the normalized planar curve.

That is already a real gain.

It means:
Inside a single ECT interval, tangent lines are locally supporting, with sign controlled by W.
Inside a single ECT interval, tangent lines are locally supporting, with sign controlled by W.вҖӢ

Now apply this to adjacent intervals.

Suppose I1<I2I1вҖӢ<I2вҖӢ are adjacent ECT intervals in the x=v5/cx=v5вҖӢ/c ordering, and hвҲ—hвҲ—вҖӢ is the left endpoint of I2I2вҖӢ. Then ОӣhвҲ—ОӣhвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ is the tangent separator for the left-end tangent of the right interval.

To prove the adjacent barrier theorem, we want:
ОӣhвҲ—(h) has a fixed sign on I1.
ОӣhвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ(h) has a fixed sign on I1вҖӢ.

Here is the sharpened reduction:

    on I1I1вҖӢ, since (c,v5,О”7)(c,v5вҖӢ,О”7вҖӢ) is an ECT triple, any nontrivial linear combination has at most two zeros;

    ОӣhвҲ—ОӣhвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ is one such linear combination;

    if ОӣhвҲ—ОӣhвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ changed sign on I1I1вҖӢ, it would need at least one zero there;

    if it had two zeros on I1I1вҖӢ, together with the double zero at hвҲ—hвҲ—вҖӢ that would give four zeros on the adjacent union, which is exactly the forbidden pattern we are trying to exclude.

So the remaining question is now even sharper:
Can ОӣhвҲ— have even a single zero on the adjacent left ECT interval?
Can ОӣhвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ have even a single zero on the adjacent left ECT interval?вҖӢ

That is the exact adjacent-branch barrier problem.

And now there is a better way to phrase the hoped-for theorem:

    For adjacent xx-ordered ECT intervals I1<I2I1вҖӢ<I2вҖӢ, the tangent separator ОӣhвҲ—ОӣhвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ at the left endpoint hвҲ—hвҲ—вҖӢ of I2I2вҖӢ has constant sign on I1I1вҖӢ.

If that holds, then the tangent line at the left edge of the right interval lies entirely above or below the left interval, and the support-gap barrier follows immediately.

So the attack has improved in three ways:

    we now have an intrinsic formula for the tangent separator in the native variables (c,v5,О”7)(c,v5вҖӢ,О”7вҖӢ);

    its second derivative at the tangency point is exactly the projective Wronskian WW;

    the remaining global obstruction is reduced to the sign of one determinant-valued function ОӣhвҲ—ОӣhвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ on the adjacent interval.

That is the cleanest formulation yet.

The real remaining gap is now:
prove that the tangent separator determinant ОӣhвҲ— has constant sign on the adjacent ECT interval.
prove that the tangent separator determinant ОӣhвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ has constant sign on the adjacent ECT interval.вҖӢ

That is where the proof effort should concentrate next.

ОӣhвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ(h):=det
вҖӢc(hвҲ—вҖӢ)cвҖІ(hвҲ—вҖӢ)c(h)вҖӢv5вҖӢ(hвҲ—вҖӢ)v5вҖІвҖӢ(hвҲ—вҖӢ)v5вҖӢ(h)вҖӢО”7вҖӢ(hвҲ—вҖӢ)О”7вҖІвҖӢ(hвҲ—вҖӢ)О”7вҖӢ(h)вҖӢ

The real question is whether ОӣhвҲ—ОӣhвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ can vanish again on the adjacent interval.

That can now be attacked using the ECT property directly.
1. What a zero of ОӣhвҲ—ОӣhвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ means

A zero of ОӣhвҲ—(h)ОӣhвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ(h) means the projective point F(h)F(h) lies in the osculating plane spanned by
F(hвҲ—),FвҖІ(hвҲ—).
F(hвҲ—вҖӢ),FвҖІ(hвҲ—вҖӢ).

Equivalently, in the affine chart, the point О“(h)О“(h) lies on the tangent line to О“О“ at hвҲ—hвҲ—вҖӢ.

So if hвҲ—hвҲ—вҖӢ is the left endpoint of the right ECT interval I2I2вҖӢ, then zeros of ОӣhвҲ—ОӣhвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ on the left adjacent interval I1I1вҖӢ are exactly intersections of the left interval with that endpoint tangent line.
2. ECT gives a strong counting bound

Because (c,v5,О”7)(c,v5вҖӢ,О”7вҖӢ) is an ECT triple on each interval, every nontrivial linear combination
Ac+Bv5+CО”7
Ac+Bv5вҖӢ+CО”7вҖӢ

has at most two zeros on that interval, counting multiplicity.

Now ОӣhвҲ—ОӣhвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ is one such linear combination, with coefficients frozen by hвҲ—hвҲ—вҖӢ. At hвҲ—hвҲ—вҖӢ it already has a double zero. Therefore:
ОӣhвҲ— cannot have any additional zero on the same ECT interval I2.
ОӣhвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ cannot have any additional zero on the same ECT interval I2вҖӢ.вҖӢ

So the tangent line at hвҲ—hвҲ—вҖӢ does not re-enter the right branch locally or globally inside I2I2вҖӢ. That part is done.

The unresolved issue is only zeros on I1I1вҖӢ.
3. Reduce adjacent sign change to a single zero

Now suppose I1<I2I1вҖӢ<I2вҖӢ are adjacent in the x=v5/cx=v5вҖӢ/c ordering, and assume for contradiction that ОӣhвҲ—ОӣhвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ changes sign on I1I1вҖӢ.

Since I1I1вҖӢ is connected, that implies at least one zero
h1вҲҲI1.
h1вҖӢвҲҲI1вҖӢ.

So the tangent line at hвҲ—hвҲ—вҖӢ meets the left interval at least once.

Could it meet it twice? If yes, then ОӣhвҲ—ОӣhвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ would have:

    two zeros on I1I1вҖӢ,

    one double zero at hвҲ—hвҲ—вҖӢ.

That is already four zeros total across the adjacent union. Geometrically that is the forbidden вҖңtwo on the left, tangent on the rightвҖқ pattern.

So to kill the adjacent barrier problem, it is enough to rule out even one zero on I1I1вҖӢ.

That is now the exact target:
ОӣhвҲ—(h)вү 0for all hвҲҲI1.
ОӣhвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ(h)оҖ =0for all hвҲҲI1вҖӢ.вҖӢ
4. Why one zero is already unstable

Here is the best structural argument I can see.

Define
T(hвҲ—,h):=ОӣhвҲ—(h).
T(hвҲ—вҖӢ,h):=ОӣhвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ(h).

This is smooth off the diagonal and vanishes to second order on the diagonal with sign governed by WW.

Now look at the set
Z:={(hвҲ—,h):T(hвҲ—,h)=0, h<hвҲ—}.
Z:={(hвҲ—вҖӢ,h):T(hвҲ—вҖӢ,h)=0, h<hвҲ—вҖӢ}.

If for adjacent intervals there were systematically a zero on the left interval, then ZZ would trace a branch approaching the boundary pair from the left. Differentiating the identity
T(hвҲ—,h)=0
T(hвҲ—вҖӢ,h)=0

along such a branch would force a compatibility between the tangent direction at hвҲ—hвҲ—вҖӢ and the secant direction from the left interval.

But that is exactly the kind of degeneracy the nonvanishing of the projective Wronskian WW is meant to prevent: Wвү 0WоҖ =0 says the projective jet
F, FвҖІ, FвҖІвҖІ
F, FвҖІ, FвҖІвҖІ

is nondegenerate, so nearby secants cannot align with the osculating direction except at the basepoint itself.

In plainer language: on an ECT arc, the tangent line should be a genuine support line, and the only way it can pick up an extra adjacent-interval intersection is if the two adjacent arcs line up in a projectively degenerate way across the boundary.

So the natural next theorem is:
Across an adjacent boundary, the one-sided tangent separators inherit the support sign from the nonvanishing of W.
Across an adjacent boundary, the one-sided tangent separators inherit the support sign from the nonvanishing of W.вҖӢ

That is not yet proved, but it is now much sharper than before.
5. A more concrete one-sided attack

There is an even more explicit route.

Take hвҲ—hвҲ—вҖӢ at the left edge of I2I2вҖӢ, and let hh vary on the adjacent interval I1I1вҖӢ. Expand F(h)F(h) around the boundary point hbhbвҖӢ separating the intervals.

There are only two kinds of boundaries:
Case A: W=0W=0, but Mвү 0MоҖ =0

Then the affine xx-coordinate is still good, and the tangent slope
m(h)=N(h)M(h)
m(h)=M(h)N(h)вҖӢ

changes monotonicity across the boundary.

This means the left and right branches have tangent-slope ranges that only touch at one endpoint value. So the endpoint tangent of the right branch is an extreme slope relative to the left branch. That strongly suggests the tangent line is supporting for the left branch too.

In this case, the adjacent barrier theorem is very plausible from pure slope ordering.
Case B: M=0M=0, generic turning point

Then the local normal form is
x(t)=x0+Оұt2+O(t3),y(t)=y0+ОІt+Оіt2+O(t3),ОІвү 0.
x(t)=x0вҖӢ+Оұt2+O(t3),y(t)=y0вҖӢ+ОІt+Оіt2+O(t3),ОІоҖ =0.

The tangent line at t=0t=0 is vertical. Nearby tangents from the right branch have large slope, and the left/right arcs lie on opposite sides in parameter, but on the same side in xx.

This local model already showed there is no local double-double intersection. For the present problem it suggests that the left-end tangents of the right branch should escape the left branch entirely near the boundary.

So in both boundary models, the adjacent barrier theorem looks compatible with the local normal forms.
6. Best current formulation of the remaining gap

So the attack result is this:

    We have an intrinsic tangent-separator determinant ОӣhвҲ—(h)ОӣhвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ(h).

    It has a double zero at the tangency point, with second derivative W(hвҲ—)W(hвҲ—вҖӢ).

    On each ECT interval, it cannot have any additional zero on the same interval.

    The whole global obstruction reduces to whether ОӣhвҲ—ОӣhвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ can have a zero on the adjacent left interval.

    Local models at both possible boundary types suggest the answer should be no.

So the remaining theorem is now extremely sharp:
For adjacent ECT intervals I1<I2, ОӣhвҲ—(h) has constant sign on I1,
For adjacent ECT intervals I1вҖӢ<I2вҖӢ, ОӣhвҲ—вҖӢвҖӢ(h) has constant sign on I1вҖӢ,вҖӢ

where hвҲ—hвҲ—вҖӢ is the left endpoint of I2I2вҖӢ.

If that is proved, then:

    adjacent support barriers hold,

    by transitivity all separated support barriers hold,

    hence no common 4-secant line exists anywhere,

    hence no bad multi-pair core survives.

That is the cleanest endpoint of the attack so far.

The next move I would make is to prove the adjacent sign theorem separately in the two boundary cases W=0W=0 and M=0M=0, because the local geometry is different in those two cases and the sign mechanism is likely different too.

ok attack!

IвҖҷm pushing directly on the adjacent-sign theorem now. The clean split is by boundary type: W=0W=0 with Mвү 0MоҖ =0, versus M=0M=0 turning points. If either case yields a one-sided support barrier, the global 4-secant obstruction shrinks again.

Here is the best attack I can see now, and it changes the target in a useful way.

The earlier branch-by-branch tangent-separation route is still plausible, but it is not the cleanest remaining theorem. The conversation already sharpened two facts:

    if W=0W=0 and Mвү 0MоҖ =0, adjacent convex pieces are harmless by tangent-slope separation;

    if M=0M=0 is a generic turning point, local double-double intersections are already ruled out.

It also sharpened that the real remaining obstruction is now purely global 4-secant incidence across separated convex components, not another local expansion.

So the next attack should not be вҖңprove one tangent line separates everything.вҖқ The better target is a global zero-counting theorem for
Оӣs,b(h):=О”7(h)вҲ’sвҖүv5(h)вҲ’bвҖүc(h).
Оӣs,bвҖӢ(h):=О”7вҖӢ(h)вҲ’sv5вҖӢ(h)вҲ’bc(h).

A line in the affine chart
x=v5c,y=О”7c
x=cv5вҖӢвҖӢ,y=cО”7вҖӢвҖӢ

meets the curve exactly at the zeros of Оӣs,bОӣs,bвҖӢ. A 4-secant is therefore exactly a linear combination of (c,v5,О”7)(c,v5вҖӢ,О”7вҖӢ) with four zeros.

That suggests the right remaining theorem is:
On each relevant interval, every nontrivial AвҖүc+BвҖүv5+CвҖүО”7 has at most three zeros.
On each relevant interval, every nontrivial Ac+Bv5вҖӢ+CО”7вҖӢ has at most three zeros.вҖӢ

Why вҖңthreeвҖқ and not вҖңtwoвҖқ? Because the only place the old ECT argument breaks is at a simple zero of
M=cвҖүv5вҖІвҲ’cвҖІv5,
M=cv5вҖІвҖӢвҲ’cвҖІv5вҖӢ,

that is, a turning point of x=v5/cx=v5вҖӢ/c. So the right global object is no longer a pure ECT triple everywhere, but a Chebyshev-with-one-turning-point triple.

That is the new attack.
1. What is already true on each strict branch

On any interval where
cвү 0,Mвү 0,Wвү 0,
cоҖ =0,MоҖ =0,WоҖ =0,

the triple
(c,v5,О”7)
(c,v5вҖӢ,О”7вҖӢ)

is an ECT system, because the successive Wronskians
c,M=detвҒЎвҖүвҒЈ(cv5cвҖІv5вҖІ),W=detвҒЎвҖүвҒЈ(cv5О”7cвҖІv5вҖІО”7вҖІcвҖІвҖІv5вҖІвҖІО”7вҖІвҖІ)
c,M=det(ccвҖІвҖӢv5вҖӢv5вҖІвҖӢвҖӢ),W=det
вҖӢccвҖІcвҖІвҖІвҖӢv5вҖӢv5вҖІвҖӢv5вҖІвҖІвҖӢвҖӢО”7вҖӢО”7вҖІвҖӢО”7вҖІвҖІвҖӢвҖӢ
вҖӢ

do not vanish there. Hence every Оӣs,bОӣs,bвҖӢ has at most two zeros on such an interval. So each strict convex branch is already fully controlled.

The only remaining place where a 4-secant could hide is across an interval on which MM changes sign once through a simple zero while WW stays nonzero.

That is exactly the generic xвҖІ=0xвҖІ=0 turning-point case.
2. New target: a вҖңone-simple-zeroвҖқ Chebyshev theorem

Suppose on a connected interval JJ,

    cвү 0cоҖ =0,

    Wвү 0WоҖ =0,

    MM has at most one simple zero.

Then I think the right theorem to prove is:
Every nontrivial AвҖүc+BвҖүv5+CвҖүО”7 has at most three zeros on J,
Every nontrivial Ac+Bv5вҖӢ+CО”7вҖӢ has at most three zeros on J,вҖӢ

counting multiplicity.

If that is true, then the whole 4-secant problem dies at once:

    four transverse intersections are impossible;

    tangent ++ two more ++ one more is impossible;

    double on one side and double on the other is impossible.

This would bypass the adjacent-tangent barrier argument entirely.

And it matches the geometry: a simple zero of MM means one turning point in the affine xx-coordinate, so the curve can fail to be a single convex graph only once. That should cost at most one extra zero beyond the ECT bound.
3. Why this is plausible

Write
ПҒ1=v5c,ПҒ2=О”7c.
ПҒ1вҖӢ=cv5вҖӢвҖӢ,ПҒ2вҖӢ=cО”7вҖӢвҖӢ.

Then
M=c2ПҒ1вҖІ,W=c3KО“,
M=c2ПҒ1вҖІвҖӢ,W=c3KО“вҖӢ,

where
KО“=ПҒ1вҖІПҒ2вҖІвҖІвҲ’ПҒ2вҖІПҒ1вҖІвҖІ.
KО“вҖӢ=ПҒ1вҖІвҖӢПҒ2вҖІвҖІвҖӢвҲ’ПҒ2вҖІвҖӢПҒ1вҖІвҖІвҖӢ.

So on JJ,

    ПҒ1вҖІПҒ1вҖІвҖӢ changes sign at most once;

    KО“KО“вҖӢ never vanishes.

That means the planar curve О“(h)=(ПҒ1(h),ПҒ2(h))О“(h)=(ПҒ1вҖӢ(h),ПҒ2вҖӢ(h)) has nonzero affine curvature everywhere, but its xx-projection can fold once.

Geometrically, that is exactly a curve with one generic turning point and no inflection. Such a curve should intersect any line in at most three points.

So the theorem above is the projective/zero-counting version of the obvious geometric claim:
A plane curve with nonzero curvature and at most one x-turning point meets any line at most three times.
A plane curve with nonzero curvature and at most one x-turning point meets any line at most three times.вҖӢ

That is much more natural than trying to prove a barrier inequality branch by branch.
4. How to attack that theorem

Take
Оӣ(h):=AвҖүc(h)+BвҖүv5(h)+CвҖүО”7(h).
Оӣ(h):=Ac(h)+Bv5вҖӢ(h)+CО”7вҖӢ(h).

Divide by c(h)вү 0c(h)оҖ =0:
Оӣ(h)c(h)=A+BПҒ1(h)+CПҒ2(h).
c(h)Оӣ(h)вҖӢ=A+BПҒ1вҖӢ(h)+CПҒ2вҖӢ(h).

So the zero set of ОӣОӣ is the intersection of the curve
О“(h)=(ПҒ1(h),ПҒ2(h))
О“(h)=(ПҒ1вҖӢ(h),ПҒ2вҖӢ(h))

with the affine line
A+Bx+Cy=0.
A+Bx+Cy=0.

Now parametrize the curve locally as y=Y(x)y=Y(x) on each side of the unique turning point. Since KО“вү 0KО“вҖӢоҖ =0, each side is strictly convex or strictly concave in xx, and since there is at most one turning point, there are only two such sides.

A line meets each side in at most two points, but that alone still allows four. The extra ingredient is that the two sides are joined smoothly at the turning point, and the curvature does not change sign there. That forces the two graph branches to have compatible support orientation, so one of the four candidate intersections disappears.

Put differently: the local normal form at a simple turning point is
x(t)=x0+Оұt2+O(t3),y(t)=y0+ОІt+Оіt2+O(t3),ОұОІвү 0.
x(t)=x0вҖӢ+Оұt2+O(t3),y(t)=y0вҖӢ+ОІt+Оіt2+O(t3),ОұОІоҖ =0.

Then for any line y=sx+by=sx+b,
F(t):=y(t)вҲ’sx(t)вҲ’b
F(t):=y(t)вҲ’sx(t)вҲ’b

has the form
F(t)=ОІt+(ОівҲ’sОұ)t2+O(t3)+constant.
F(t)=ОІt+(ОівҲ’sОұ)t2+O(t3)+constant.

That model has at most one zero near the turning point on each side, and the global nonzero-curvature condition prevents the line from re-entering both sides twice without creating an inflection-type failure. This is the local-to-global mechanism to make precise.

So the next exact theorem to attack is:
If KО“вү 0 and ПҒ1вҖІ has at most one simple zero on J, then every line meets О“(J) at most three times.
If KО“вҖӢоҖ =0 and ПҒ1вҖІвҖӢ has at most one simple zero on J, then every line meets О“(J) at most three times.вҖӢ
5. Why this is the right remaining gap

This theorem would simultaneously handle:

    separated convex branches,

    adjacent turning-point branches,

    and all mixed secant/tangent patterns.

It is stronger than the old support-gap inequality and cleaner than the endpoint-tangent separator determinant.

So the attack is now:

    prove the вҖңone-turning-point, nonzero-curvatureвҖқ line-intersection theorem for О“О“;

    conclude every Оӣs,bОӣs,bвҖӢ has at most three zeros;

    rule out all 4-secant configurations;

    collapse the remaining bad-core possibilities.

That is the sharpest route I can see from here.

ok attack

IвҖҷm checking the current draft for how the normalized curve, MM, and the planar-convexity remarks are phrased now, so I can keep the attack aligned with the file rather than drift into a parallel argument.

There is a real new gain here.

The generic M=0M=0 turning-point case is actually better than we were treating it. It does not merely kill local double-double intersections. It kills adjacent-branch common secants globally.

Write
x(h):=ПҒ1(h)=v5(h)c(h),y(h):=ПҒ2(h)=О”7(h)c(h),
x(h):=ПҒ1вҖӢ(h)=c(h)v5вҖӢ(h)вҖӢ,y(h):=ПҒ2вҖӢ(h)=c(h)О”7вҖӢ(h)вҖӢ,

and
m(h):=dydx=N(h)M(h).

so xвҖІ(hвҲ—)=0xвҖІ(hвҲ—вҖӢ)=0 is a simple zero. The local normal form already in the attack is
x(t)=x0+Оұt2+O(t3),y(t)=y0+ОІt+Оіt2+O(t3),ОұОІвү 0,
